title: Aberrant Dragonmark
contents: property | Type/Prerequisites | Dragonmark Feat (Eberron, Only Dragonmark)
rule
text | You gain the following benefits.

description | Aberrant Fortitude | When you fail a Constitution saving throw, you can take a Reaction to roll 1d4 and add the number rolled to the save, potentially turning the failure into a success. Once you've used this benefit, you can't use it again until you finish a Long Rest.

description | Aberrant Magic | You know one cantrip of your choice from the Sorcerer spell list. Also, choose a level 1 spell from that spell list. You always have that spell prepared. You can cast it once without a spell slot, and you regain the ability to cast it in that way when you finish a Short or Long Rest. You can also cast this spell using any spell slots you have. Constitution is your spellcasting ability for this spell.

description | Aberrant Surge | When you cast the level 1 spell from this feat, you can expend one of your Hit Point Dice and roll it. If you roll an even number, you gain a number of Temporary Hit Points equal to the number rolled. If you roll an odd number, one creature within 30 feet of you (not including you) takes Force damage equal to the number rolled. If no other creatures are in range, you take the damage.

title: Ability Score Improvement
contents: property | Type/Prerequisites | General Feat (Lvl 4)
rule
text | Increase one ability score of your choice by 2, or increase two ability scores of your choice by 1. This feat can't increase an ability score above 20.
description | Repeatable | You can take this feat more than once.

title: Actor
contents: property | Type/Prerequisites | General Feat (Lvl 4, Cha 13+)
rule
text | You gain the following benefits.
description | Ability Score Increase | Increase your Charisma score by 1, to a maximum of 20.
description | Impersonation | While you're disguised as a real or fictional person, you have Advantage on Charisma (Deception or Performance) checks to convince others that you are that person.

description | Mimicry | You can mimic the sounds of other creatures, including speech. A creature that hears the mimicry must succeed on a Wisdom (Insight) check to determine the effect is faked (DC 8 plus your Charisma modifier and Proficiency Bonus).

title: Adept of the Black Robes
contents: property | Type/Prerequisites | Lvl 4, Initiate of High Sorcery
rule
text | You chose the moon Nuitari to influence your magic, and your ambition and loyalty to the Order of the Black Robes have been recognized, granting you these benefits:

bullet | Ambitious Magic. You learn one 2nd-level spell of your choice. The 2nd-level spell must be from the enchantment or necromancy school of magic. You can cast this feat's 2nd-level spell without a spell slot, and you must finish a long rest before you can cast it in this way again. You can also cast this spell using spell slots you have of the appropriate level. The spell's spellcasting ability is the one chosen when you gained the Initiate of High Sorcery feat.

bullet | Life Channel. You can channel your life force into the power of your magic. When a creature you can see within 60 feet of you fails a saving throw against a spell that deals damage that you cast, you can expend a number of Hit Dice equal to the level of the spell. Roll the expended Hit Dice and add them together. The damage that the creature takes increases by an amount equal to that total.

title: Adept of the Red Robes
contents: property | Type/Prerequisites | Lvl 4, Initiate of High Sorcery
rule

text | You chose the moon Lunitari to influence your magic, and your dedication to maintaining the balance between all things has been recognized by the Order of the Red Robes, granting you these benefits:

bullet | Insightful Magic. You learn one 2nd-level spell of your choice. The 2nd-level spell must be from the illusion or transmutation school of magic. You can cast this feat's 2nd-level spell without a spell slot, and you must finish a long rest before you can cast it in this way again. You can also cast this spell using spell slots you have of the appropriate level. The spell's spellcasting ability is the one chosen when you gained the Initiate of High Sorcery feat.

bullet | Magical Balance. When you make an attack roll or an ability check and roll a 9 or lower on the d20, you can balance fate and treat the roll as a 10. You can balance fate in this way a number of times equal to your proficiency bonus, and you regain all expended uses when you finish a long rest.

title: Adept of the White Robes
contents: property | Type/Prerequisites | Lvl 4, Initiate of High Sorcery
rule

text | You chose the moon Solinari to influence your magic, and your oath to use magic to make the world a better place has been recognized by the Order of the White Robes, granting you these benefits:

bullet | Protective Magic. You learn one 2nd-level spell of your choice. The 2nd-level spell must be from the abjuration or divination school of magic. You can cast this feat's 2nd-level spell without a spell slot, and you must finish a long rest before you can cast it in this way again. You can also cast this spell using spell slots you have of the appropriate level. The spell's spellcasting ability is the one chosen when you gained the Initiate of High Sorcery feat.

bullet | Protective Ward. When you or a creature you can see within 30 feet of you takes damage, you can use your reaction to expend a spell slot and weave protective magic around the target. Roll a number of d6s equal to the level of the spell slot expended and reduce the damage the target takes by the total rolled on those dice + your spellcasting ability modifier.

title: Adroit Crafter
contents: property | Type/Prerequisites | Prof skill/tool
rule
text | You've trained in crafting items using a skill or tool that you're proficient with, and the steps in crafting have become second nature to you. You gain the following benefits:
bullet | The time it takes to create an item using your proficient skills or tools is halved.
bullet | If you fail to successfully craft the item and would lose the components, you retain the components instead.

title: Advanced Weapon Proficiency
contents: property | Type/Prerequisites | Fighting Style Feat (Fighting Style, Special)
rule
text | You gain the following benefits.
description | Weapon Proficiency | You have proficiency with Advanced weapons, and your training allows you to use the mastery properties of those weapons.
description | Special | This feat can be taken as a General feat by a character of level 8 or higher.

title: Aerial Expert
contents: property | Type/Prerequisites | Special
rule
text | Years of practice or an innate talent have made you adept at gliding. You gain the following benefits:

bullet | You no longer need to move at least 10 feet to perform long and high jumps. You may choose whether the jump uses your Strength or Dexterity score for determining height or distance, and you double the distance you would normally leap in either case.
bullet | You may take the Dash action while gliding to fly an additional distance up to your movement speed.
bullet | You may change direction freely while gliding, and may gain up to 10 feet of altitude once before you finish your descent.

title: Against the Unseen
contents: property | Type/Prerequisites | Virtues of the Elves Feat (Elven)
rule

text | Elves can perceive creatures that dwell in the wraith-world, be they spirits or ghosts, even when they are normally invisible to the eyes of the living and appear only as a chilling whisper of voices. You have learnt to strengthen your heart against such terrors.
bullet | Increase your Wisdom or Charisma by 1, to a maximum of 20.
bullet | You have advantage on Charisma saving throws, and you cannot be frightened by Undead creatures.

title: Agent of Order
contents: property | Type/Prerequisites | Lvl 4, Scion of the Outer Planes
rule
text | You can channel cosmic forces of order to gain these benefits:
bullet | Ability Score Increase. Increase one ability score of your choice by 1, to a maximum of 20.

bullet | Stasis Strike. Once per turn, when you damage a creature you can see within 60 feet of yourself, you can deal an extra 1d8 force damage to the target, and it must make a Wisdom saving throw (DC equal to 8 + your proficiency bonus + the modifier of the spellcasting ability you chose for the Scion of the Outer Planes feat) as spectral bindings try to ensnare it. On a successful save, the target escapes. On a failed save, the target has the restrained condition until the start of your next turn. These bindings manifest as chains or some other symbol of stasis. You can use this benefit a number of times equal to your proficiency bonus, and you regain all expended uses when you finish a long rest.

title: Alert
contents: property | Type/Prerequisites | Origin Feat (—)
rule
text | You gain the following benefits.
description | Initiative Proficiency | When you roll Initiative, you can add your Proficiency Bonus to the roll.

description | Initiative Swap | Immediately after you roll Initiative, you can swap your Initiative with the Initiative of one willing ally in the same combat. You can't make this swap if you or the ally has the Incapacitated condition.

title: Altered
contents: property | Type/Prerequisites | Origin Feat (—)
rule

text | You have been changed through magic, science, or a volatile blend of the two. You have a blatant physical augmentation of your choice from the options below. The augmentation is obvious—such as with stitches, grafts of other creature's body parts, or implants—unless disguised.
description | Aquatic Adaptation | You have gills or mucus membranes that allow you to breathe water, and you have webbed extremities that grant you a Swim Speed equal to your Speed.
description | Natural Armor | You have scales, plates, or thick hide that grants you an AC of 13 + your Dexterity or Constitution modifier (choose the ability when you select this feat).

description | Natural Weapons | You have claws, fangs, horns, or some other natural weapon that you can use to make Unarmed Strikes that deal 1d8 damage on a hit. The damage is Bludgeoning, Piercing, or Slashing as appropriate to the form.
description | Night Vision | You have Darkvision with a range of 60 feet. If you already have Darkvision, its range increases by 30 feet.

title: Archery
contents: property | Type/Prerequisites | Fighting Style Feat (Fighting Style)
rule
text | You gain a +2 bonus to attack rolls you make with Ranged weapons.

title: Art of Disappearing
contents: property | Type/Prerequisites | Virtues of the Hobbits Feat (Hobbit)
rule

text | Hobbits are said to have little or no magic about them, but the way they can choose to disappear quickly and quietly can be described by others only as supernatural. You have learnt to choose exactly the right moment to turn away from the attention of others, sometimes unconsciously anticipating the need to disappear.
bullet | Increase your Dexterity score by 1, to a maximum of 20.
bullet | Your proficiency bonus is doubled for any Dexterity (Stealth) check you make.
bullet | You can try to hide even when you are only lightly obscured from the creature from which you are hiding.

title: Artificer Initiate
contents: rule
text | You've learned some of an artificer's inventiveness:

bullet | You learn one cantrip of your choice from the artificer spell list, and you learn one 1st-level spell of your choice from that list. Intelligence is your spellcasting ability for these spells.

bullet | You can cast this feat's 1st-level spell without a spell slot, and you must finish a long rest before you can cast it in this way again. You can also cast the spell using any spell slots you have.

bullet | You gain proficiency with one type of artisan's tools of your choice, and you can use that type of tool as a spellcasting focus for any spell you cast that uses Intelligence as its spellcasting ability.

title: Athlete
contents: property | Type/Prerequisites | General Feat (Lvl 4 + Str 13+/Dex 13+)
rule
text | You gain the following benefits.
description | Ability Score Increase | Increase your Strength or Dexterity by 1, to a maximum of 20.
description | Climb Speed | You gain a Climb Speed equal to your Speed.
description | Hop Up | When you have the Prone condition, you can right yourself with only 5 feet of movement.
description | Jumping | You can make a running Long or High Jump after moving only 5 feet.

title: Baleful Scion
contents: property | Type/Prerequisites | Lvl 4, Scion of the Outer Planes
rule
text | You can channel cosmic forces of evil to gain these benefits:
bullet | Ability Score Increase. Increase one ability score of your choice by 1, to a maximum of 20.

bullet | Grasp of Avarice. Once per turn, when you damage a creature you can see within 60 feet of yourself, you can also deal necrotic damage to it. The necrotic damage equals 1d6 + your proficiency bonus, and you regain a number of hit points equal to this necrotic damage dealt. You can use this benefit a number of times equal to your proficiency bonus, and you regain all expended uses when you finish a long rest.

title: Bandit Cunning
contents: rule
text | Your time as a bandit has granted you a sense for danger, and made you skilled at sizing up opponents. You gain the following benefits:

bullet | When you are asked to make a saving throw, you may spend your reaction to add your Intelligence modifier as an additional bonus to the saving throw. You cannot use this ability again until you have completed a long rest.

bullet | During combat, you can use your action to make an Intelligence (Investigation) check against any creature you have seen fight. As long as you succeed against a DC of 10 + their challenge rating, you can learn one useful fact of your choice about the target's combat abilities. Choose from:
bullet | one damage resistance or immunity
bullet | one condition immunity
bullet | one special ability possessed by the creature that either does damage, or prevents damage
bullet | one option under their attack, legendary, or reaction actions
bullet | one special sense they possess

title: Baruk Khazâd!
contents: property | Type/Prerequisites | Virtues of the Dwarves Feat (Dwarven)
rule
text | The secret tongue of the Dwarves is guarded as a treasure of the past, yet their battle-cry is well-known and feared by their foes.
bullet | Increase your Strength or Charisma by 1, to a maximum of 20.

bullet | You can use a bonus action on your turn to force each hostile creature that can hear and see you within 30 feet of you to make a Charisma (Intimidation) check (DC 8 + your proficiency bonus + your choice of your Strength or Charisma modifier). On a failed check, the creature becomes frightened of you until the end of your next turn. Attack rolls against creatures frightened in this way have advantage. Once you use this virtue, you cannot use it again until you finish a short or long rest.

title: Beast-Craft
contents: property | Type/Prerequisites | Craft Feat (—)
rule

text | You gain the ability to comprehend and verbally communicate with beasts. The knowledge and awareness of many beasts is limited by their intelligence, but at minimum, beasts can give you information about nearby locations and creatures, including whatever they can perceive or have perceived within the past day. You might be able to persuade a beast to perform a small favour for you, at the Loremaster's discretion, such as delivering a letter, or a verbal message to someone who can also speak with beasts.

text | In addition, as an action you can spend a craft slot and choose a beast that you can see within 30 feet of you. It must be able to see and hear you. Make a Wisdom (Animal Handling) check, contested by the target's Wisdom (Insight) check. If you win the contest, the beast is charmed by you for 24 hours or until you or your companions do anything harmful to it. If you obtain a magical success and the target is a beast with a challenge rating of 1/8 or lower, you can make the beast permanently charmed by you, until you dismiss it, harm it, or make another beast permanently charmed by you. A beast automatically wins the contest if its Intelligence score is 4 or higher. When you reach 5th level, you can affect one additional beast, or two additional beasts at 9th level.

title: Blackpowder Pistol Expert
contents: property | Type/Prerequisites | General Feat (Lvl 4)
rule
text | You have become practiced with the use of Blackpowder Pistols. You gain the following benefits.
description | Ability Score Increase | Increase your Dexterity, Constitution, or Intelligence by 1, to a maximum of 20.
description | Deadeye | Attacking at long range doesn't impose Disadvantage on your attack rolls with a Blackpowder Pistol.
description | Quick Load | You ignore Loading of the Blackpowder Pistol.
description | Trick Shot | Immediately after a creature within 5 feet of you moves, you can take a Reaction to make a ranged attack with a Blackpowder Pistol against that creature.

title: Blessed Warrior
contents: property | Type/Prerequisites | Fighting Style Replacement (Paladin) (Special)
rule

text | You learn two Cleric cantrips of your choice. Guidance and Sacred Flame are recommended. The chosen cantrips count as Paladin spells for you, and Charisma is your spellcasting ability for them. Whenever you gain a Paladin level, you can replace one of these cantrips with another Cleric cantrip.

title: Blind Fighting
contents: property | Type/Prerequisites | Fighting Style Feat (Fighting Style)
rule
text | You have Blindsight with a range of 10 feet.

title: Blood Hound
contents: property | Type/Prerequisites | Origin Feat (—)
rule

text | Your senses are heightened beyond those of most folk. Whether these senses were strengthened through training or the loss of other senses, or they simply matured as you did, you are a master of finding those whom you seek. You  gain the following benefits.
description | Motion Sensor | Whenever a creature that is Small or larger moves within 10 feet of you while you do not have the Unconscious condition, you immediately become aware of its presence.
description | No Hiding | You have Advantage on Wisdom (Perception) checks that rely on sound or smell.

title: Bloodlust
contents: property | Type/Prerequisites | General Feat (Lvl 4)
rule
text | You gain the following benefits.
description | Ability Score Increase | Increase your Strength, Dexterity, or Constitution by 1, to a maximum of 20.
description | Powerful Recovery | When you roll a Hit Point Die to regain Hit Points, you can treat any roll of 1 or 2 as a 3.

description | Sanguine Feast | Once per turn when you hit a Bloodied creature that isn't a Construct or Undead with an attack roll, you can expend a Hit Point Die, roll it, and regain a number of Hit Points equal to the number rolled plus your Constitution modifier.
text | You can use this feature a number of times equal to your Proficiency Bonus, and you regain all expended uses when you finish a Long Rest.

title: Bomber
contents: property | Type/Prerequisites | General Feat (Lvl 4)
rule
text | You gain the following benefits.
description | Ability Score Increase | Increase your Dexterity score by 1, to a maximum of 20.
description | Far Lobber | When you use the Attack action to throw a vial or flask, you can target an object or creature you can see within 40 feet of yourself.
description | Long Shots | Attacking at long range doesn't impose Disadvantage on your attack rolls with Thrown weapons.

title: Boon of Blazing Dawn
contents: property | Type/Prerequisites | Epic Boon Feat (Lvl 19)
rule
text | You gain the following benefits.
description | Ability Score Increase | Increase one ability score of your choice by 1, to a maximum of 30.
description | Beloved of the Sun | You have Immunity to Radiant damage.
description | Blazing Strike | When you hit a creature with a weapon attack, the damage can be Radiant or the weapon's normal damage type (your choice).

description | Burst of Sunlight | Once per turn when you hit a creature with an attack that deals Radiant damage, you can emit Bright Light in a 30-foot radius from yourself and Dim Light for an additional 30 feet until the start of your next turn. This light is sunlight.

title: Boon of Bloodshed
contents: property | Type/Prerequisites | Epic Boon Feat (Lvl 19)
rule
text | You gain the following benefits.
description | Ability Score Increase | Increase one ability score of your choice by 1, to a maximum of 30.
description | Killer's Fortune | When an enemy you can see is reduced to 0 Hit Points, you gain Advantage on the next attack roll you make before the end of your next turn.

description | Power from Pain | Once per turn, when you make an attack roll while Bloodied, you can deal extra damage to the target equal to your Proficiency Bonus. The extra damage's type is the same as the attack's type.

title: Boon of Bountiful Health
contents: property | Type/Prerequisites | Epic Boon Feat (Lvl 19)
rule
text | You gain the following benefits.
description | Ability Score Increase | Increase one ability score of your choice by 1, to a maximum of 30.
description | Augmented Health | When you gain Temporary Hit Points, increase the number of Temporary Hit Points you gain by 5.
description | Superior Recuperation | When you spend one or more Hit Point Dice to regain Hit Points, you can instead use the highest number possible for each die.

title: Boon of Combat Prowess
contents: property | Type/Prerequisites | Epic Boon Feat (Lvl 19)
rule
text | You gain the following benefits.
description | Ability Score Increase | Increase one ability score of your choice by 1, to a maximum of 30.
description | Peerless Aim | When you miss with an attack roll, you can hit instead. Once you use this benefit, you can't use it again until the start of your next turn.

title: Boon of Communication
contents: property | Type/Prerequisites | Epic Boon Feat (Lvl 19)
rule
text | You gain the following benefits.
description | Ability Score Increase | Increase your Intelligence, Wisdom, or Charisma by 1, to a maximum of 30.
description | Cunning Speaker | You don't have Disadvantage on ability checks to influence Hostile creatures.
description | Gifted Interpreter | You understand the literal meaning of any language you hear or see signed, and you can understand the literal meaning of any written language you see.
description | Mental Communication | You gain telepathy with a range of 120 feet.

title: Boon of Desperate Resilience
contents: property | Type/Prerequisites | Epic Boon Feat (Lvl 19)
rule
text | You gain the following benefits.
description | Ability Score Increase | Increase your Strength or Constitution by 1, to a maximum of 30.
description | Defense of Body and Mind | While you are Bloodied, you have Resistance to every damage type except Force.

title: Boon of Dimensional Travel
contents: property | Type/Prerequisites | Epic Boon Feat (Lvl 19)
rule
text | You gain the following benefits.
description | Ability Score Increase | Increase one ability score of your choice by 1, to a maximum of 30.
description | Blink Steps | Immediately after you take the Attack action or the Magic action, you can teleport up to 30 feet to an unoccupied space you can see.

title: Boon of Energy Resistance
contents: property | Type/Prerequisites | Epic Boon Feat (Lvl 19)
rule
text | You gain the following benefits.
description | Ability Score Increase | Increase one ability score of your choice by 1, to a maximum of 30.

description | Energy Resistances | You gain Resistance to two of the following damage types of your choice: Acid, Cold, Fire, Lightning, Necrotic, Poison, Psychic, Radiant, or Thunder. Whenever you finish a Long Rest, you can change your choices.

description | Energy Redirection | When you take damage of one of the types chosen for the Energy Resistances benefit, you can take a Reaction to direct damage of the same type toward another creature you can see within 60 feet of yourself that isn't behind Total Cover. If you do so, that creature must succeed on a Dexterity saving throw (DC 8 plus your Constitution modifier and Proficiency Bonus) or take damage equal to 2d12 plus your Constitution modifier.

title: Boon of Exquisite Radiance
contents: property | Type/Prerequisites | Epic Boon Feat (Lvl 19)
rule
text | You gain the following benefits.
description | Ability Score Increase | Increase one ability score of your choice by 1, to a maximum of 30.
description | Eternal Rest | Creatures you reduce to 0 Hit Points can't become Undead.

description | Powerful Radiance | When you make a damage roll that deals Radiant damage, you can instead use the highest number possible for each damage die. Once you use this benefit, you can't do so again until you finish a Long Rest.

title: Boon of Fate
contents: property | Type/Prerequisites | Epic Boon Feat (Lvl 19)
rule
text | You gain the following benefits.
description | Ability Score Increase | Increase one ability score of your choice by 1, to a maximum of 30.

description | Improve Fate | When you or another creature within 60 feet of you succeeds on or fails a D20 Test, you can roll 2d4 and apply the total rolled as a bonus or penalty to the d20 roll. Once you use this benefit, you can't use it again until you roll Initiative or finish a Short or Long Rest.

title: Boon of Fluid Forms
contents: property | Type/Prerequisites | Epic Boon Feat (Lvl 19)
rule
text | You gain the following benefits.
description | Ability Score Increase | Increase your Intelligence, Wisdom, or Charisma by 1, to a maximum of 30.

description | Shapechanger | You can take a Magic action to shape-shift into a Beast, Humanoid, or Monstrosity with a Challenge Rating no higher than 10. When you shape-shift, you gain a number of Temporary Hit Points equal to the Hit Points of the form. The shape-shifting effect lasts for 1 hour, and it ends early if you have no Temporary Hit Points left or if you take a Magic action to return to your true form.

text | Your game statistics are replaced by the stat block of the chosen form, but you retain your creature type; alignment; personality; Intelligence, Wisdom, and Charisma scores; Hit Points; Hit Point Dice; proficiencies; and ability to communicate. If you have the Spellcasting or Pact Magic feature, you retain it too. Upon shape-shifting, you determine whether your equipment drops to the ground or changes in size and shape to fit the new form while you're in it.
text | Once you use this benefit, you can't do so again until you finish a Long Rest.
description | Hardy Transformation | When you gain Temporary Hit Points when you shape-shift, increase that number of Temporary Hit Points by 20.

title: Boon of Fortitude
contents: property | Type/Prerequisites | Epic Boon Feat (Lvl 19)
rule
text | You gain the following benefits.
description | Ability Score Increase | Increase one ability score of your choice by 1, to a maximum of 30.

description | Fortified Health | Your Hit Point maximum increases by 40. In addition, whenever you regain Hit Points, you can regain additional Hit Points equal to your Constitution modifier. Once you've regained these additional Hit Points, you can't do so again until the start of your next turn.

title: Boon of Fortune's Favor
contents: property | Type/Prerequisites | Epic Boon Feat (Lvl 19)
rule
text | You gain the following benefits.
description | Ability Score Increase | Increase one ability score of your choice by 1, to a maximum of 30.
description | Saving Throw Reroll | When you fail a saving throw, you can reroll it and must use the new roll. Once you use this benefit, you can't do so again until the start of your next turn.

title: Boon of Irresistible Offense
contents: property | Type/Prerequisites | Epic Boon Feat (Lvl 19)
rule
text | You gain the following benefits.
description | Ability Score Increase | Increase your Strength or Dexterity by 1, to a maximum of 30.
description | Overcome Defenses | The Bludgeoning, Piercing, and Slashing damage you deal always ignores Resistance.

description | Overwhelming Strike | When you roll a 20 on the d20 for an attack roll, you can deal extra damage to the target equal to the ability score increased by this feat. The extra damage's type is the same as the attack's type.

title: Boon of Looming Shadows
contents: property | Type/Prerequisites | Epic Boon Feat (Lvl 19)
rule
text | You gain the following benefits.
description | Ability Score Increase | Increase one ability score of your choice by 1, to a maximum of 30.
description | Shadowy Stretch | When you take the Attack action, your reach for your Melee weapon attacks increases by 10 feet until the end of your turn.
description | Dancing Silhouette | You can take the Dodge action as a Bonus Action.

title: Boon of Magic Resistance
contents: property | Type/Prerequisites | Epic Boon Feat (Lvl 19)
rule
text | You gain the following benefits.
description | Ability Score Increase | Increase your Intelligence or Wisdom by 1, to a maximum of 30.
description | Heroic Resistance | If you fail a saving throw, you can cause yourself to succeed instead. Once you use this benefit, you can't use it again until you finish a Short or Long Rest.

title: Boon of Misty Escape
contents: property | Type/Prerequisites | Epic Boon Feat (Lvl 19)
rule
text | You gain the following benefits.
description | Ability Score Increase | Increase your Intelligence, Wisdom, or Charisma by 1, to a maximum of 30.

description | Gaseous Form | If you drop to 0 Hit Points but aren't killed outright, you can instead drop to 1 Hit Point and cast Gaseous Form without expending a spell slot (no action required). When you cast this spell this way, you can target only yourself, your Fly Speed is 20 feet, and you regain 10 Hit Points at the start of each of your turns for the spell's duration. The spell's spellcasting ability is the ability increased by this feat.
text | Once you use this benefit, you can't do so again until you finish a Long Rest.

title: Boon of Perfect Flight
contents: property | Type/Prerequisites | Epic Boon Feat (Lvl 19)
rule
text | You gain the following benefits.
description | Ability Score Increase | Increase your Strength or Dexterity by 1, to a maximum of 30.
description | Flying | You have a Fly Speed of 40 feet and can hover.
description | Graceful Fall | If you fall more than 5 feet, your rate of descent slows to 60 feet per round until you land.

title: Boon of Poison Mastery
contents: property | Type/Prerequisites | Epic Boon Feat (Lvl 19)
rule
text | You gain the following benefits.
description | Ability Score Increase | Increase one ability score of your choice by 1, to a maximum of 30.
description | Antitoxic | You have Immunity to Poison damage and the Poisoned condition.

description | Perfect Poisoner | Once per turn, when you roll dice to determine Poison damage a creature takes from your attack, spell, or feature, you can instead use the highest number possible for each die.

title: Boon of Recovery
contents: property | Type/Prerequisites | Epic Boon Feat (Lvl 19)
rule
text | You gain the following benefits.
description | Ability Score Increase | Increase one ability score of your choice by 1, to a maximum of 30.

description | Last Stand | When you would be reduced to 0 Hit Points, you can drop to 1 Hit Point instead and regain a number of Hit Points equal to half your Hit Point maximum. Once you use this benefit, you can't use it again until you finish a Long Rest.

description | Recover Vitality | You have a pool of ten d10s. As a Bonus Action, you can expend dice from the pool, roll those dice, and regain a number of Hit Points equal to the roll's total. You regain all the expended dice when you finish a Long Rest.

title: Boon of Revelry
contents: property | Type/Prerequisites | Epic Boon Feat (Lvl 19)
rule
text | You gain the following benefits.
description | Ability Score Increase | Increase your Intelligence, Wisdom, or Charisma by 1, to a maximum of 30.

description | Inspire Dance | You always have the Otto's Irresistible Dance spell prepared. You can cast it once without a spell slot, and you regain the ability to cast it that way when you finish a Long Rest. You can also cast the spell using any spell slots you have of the appropriate level.
text | When you cast the spell, it requires no spell components, and taking damage doesn't break your Concentration on it.

description | Sing Out | While a creature that failed its saving throw against your Otto's Irresistible Dance has the Charmed condition from that spell, it can't cast spells with Verbal components, and it sings delightful nonsense if it can sing.

title: Boon of Shadowsteel Mastery
contents: property | Type/Prerequisites | Epic Boon Feat (Lvl 19, Special)
rule
text | You gain the following benefits.

If you do so, the creature must succeed on a Wisdom saving throw (DC 8 plus your Charisma modifier and your Proficiency Bonus) or spend its turn moving away from you by the fastest available means. Once you use this benefit, you can't use it again until you finish a Short or Long Rest.
description | Intimidating | You gain Proficiency in the Intimidation skill if you don't already have it. You also gain Expertise in Intimidation.

If any of the Emanation's area overlaps with an area of Darkness created by a spell, that spell is dispelled. The Emanation lasts until you dismiss it (no action required), die, or have the Incapacitated condition.
description | Fortifying Light | When your Daylight Presence is active, at the start of each of your turns, you and allies you can see in your Daylight Presence gain 10 Temporary Hit Points.

description | Storm's Strength | Creatures have Disadvantage on saving throws against your spells that deal Lightning or Thunder damage.

contents: property | Type/Prerequisites | Epic Boon Feat (Lvl 19)
rule
text | You gain the following benefits.
description | Ability Score Increase | Increase one ability score of your choice by 1, to a maximum of 30.

description | Merge with Shadows | While within Dim Light or Darkness, you can give yourself the Invisible condition as a Bonus Action. The condition ends on you immediately after you take an action, a Bonus Action, or a Reaction.
description | Shadowy Form | While within Dim Light or Darkness, you have Resistance to all damage except Psychic and Radiant.

description | Siphon Life | When an enemy within 120 feet of you is reduced to 0 Hit Points, you can take a Reaction to regain 50 Hit Points. Once you use this benefit, you can't use it again until you finish a Short or Long Rest.

At the end of each of your turns while in Hybrid Form, if you have no Temporary Hit Points, you gain 10 Temporary Hit Points. While you are in your Hybrid Form and have no Temporary Hit Points, you have Advantage on attack rolls.

title: Boon of Truesight

text | You gain the following benefits.
description | Ability Score Increase | Increase one ability score of your choice by 1, to a maximum of 30.

title: Bountiful Luck
contents: property | Type/Prerequisites | Halfling
rule

text | Your people have extraordinary luck, which you have learned to mystically lend to your companions when you see them falter. You're not sure how you do it; you just wish it, and it happens. Surely a sign of fortune's favor!

text | When an ally you can see within 30 feet of you rolls a 1 on the d20 for an attack roll, an ability check, or a saving throw, you can use your reaction to let the ally reroll the die. The ally must use the new roll.
text | When you use this ability, you can't use your Lucky racial trait before the end of your next turn.

title: Brave at a Pinch
contents: property | Type/Prerequisites | Virtues of the Hobbits Feat (Hobbit)
rule

text | One of the Wise once said that you have to put a Hobbit in a tight place before you find out what is in them. You prefer to avoid finding yourself in one, but you are also sure you would be able to take good care of yourself in the circumstances.
bullet | Increase one ability score of your choice by 1, to a maximum of 20.

bullet | Whenever a hostile creature or harmful effect imposes a condition on you, you suffer one or more levels of exhaustion, or you drop to half or less of your maximum hit points, you gain inspiration.

title: Bree-Pony
contents: property | Type/Prerequisites | Virtues of the Men of Bree Feat (Men of Bree/Bree-Hobbit)
rule

text | Bree-ponies are very similar to their owners, the Bree-folk—they don't seem to have anything special compared to their peers in other parts of Middle-earth, and yet some of them display uncommon courage and exceptional memory. You have acquired an unusually brave and intelligent beast that follows you everywhere.
bullet | Increase your Wisdom score by 1, to a maximum of 20.
bullet | Your pony uses your proficiency bonus rather than its own, and adds your proficiency bonus to each of its ability scores, AC, and to any ability check or saving throw that it makes.
bullet | Your pony has a number of Hit Dice equal to your character level instead of its normal Hit Dice (minimum 2).

bullet | Your pony obeys your commands. In combat, it takes its turn on your initiative and acts and moves as you direct it (no action required by you), or on its own if you are absent or incapacitated. It cannot make opportunity attacks, and its only action options are Dash, Disengage, and Dodge, unless you spend an action on your turn to command it to take another action. If your pony dies, you can acquire a new one during the next Yule Fellowship Phase.

title: Broken Spells
contents: property | Type/Prerequisites | Virtues of the Dwarves Feat (Dwarven)
rule
text | In ancient times, the Dwarves made mighty spells. You have been taught fragments of old enchantments that retain some power to this day.
bullet | Increase one ability score of your choice by 1, to a maximum of 20.

bullet | Choose any combination of three skills or tools you are proficient with. Whenever you make an ability check that uses one of the chosen proficiencies, you can obtain a magical success. You can do so a number of times equal to your proficiency bonus, and you regain all expended uses when you finish a long rest.

title: Brutal Grip
contents: property | Type/Prerequisites | General Feat (Lvl 4, Str 13+)
rule
text | You gain the following benefits.
description | Ability Score Increase | Increase your Strength score by 1, to a maximum of 20.
description | Heavy Duelist | You can wield a Melee weapon with Two-Handed in one hand.
description | Versatile Dual Wielder | While wielding a Melee weapon with Versatile in one hand, the weapon has the Light property for you.

title: Careful Crafter
contents: property | Type/Prerequisites | Prof skill/tool
rule
text | You've trained in crafting items using a skill or tool that you're proficient with, and your creations are known for being well made and reliable. You gain the following benefits:
bullet | You make all ability checks for crafting with advantage.
bullet | Anyone assisting you in crafting an item gains advantage on their ability checks for crafting.
bullet | If you fail to successfully craft the item, the amount of time it took to complete the process is halved.

title: Cartomancer
contents: property | Type/Prerequisites | Lvl 4, Spellcasting
rule
text | You have learned to channel your magic through a deck of cards. You can use a card deck as your spellcasting focus, and you gain the following benefits:

bullet | Card Tricks. You learn the Prestidigitation cantrip and can use it to create illusions that duplicate the effects of stage magic. When you use Prestidigitation in this way, you can conceal the verbal and somatic components of the spell as ordinary conversation and card handling.

bullet | Hidden Ace. When you finish a long rest, you can choose one spell from your class's spell list and imbue that spell into a card. The chosen spell must have a casting time of 1 action, and it must be a level for which you have spell slots. The card remains imbued with this spell for 8 hours. While the card is imbued with the spell, you can use a bonus action to flourish the card and cast the spell within. The card then immediately loses its magic.

title: Charger
contents: property | Type/Prerequisites | General Feat (Lvl 4 + Str 13+/Dex 13+)
rule
text | You gain the following benefits.
description | Ability Score Increase | Increase your Strength or Dexterity by 1, to a maximum of 20.
description | Improved Dash | When you take the Dash action, your Speed increases by 10 feet for that action.

description | Charge Attack | If you move at least 10 feet in a straight line toward a target immediately before hitting it with a melee attack roll as part of the Attack action, choose one of the following effects: gain a 1d8 bonus to the attack's damage roll, or push the target up to 10 feet away if it is no more than one size larger than you. You can use this benefit only once on each of your turns.

title: Charm Twister
contents: property | Type/Prerequisites | Origin Feat (—)
rule
text | You know hedge remedies and hexes passed down through Old Ways teachings, and you can impart them into small wicker charms you weave. You gain the following benefits.

description | Old Blessing | You can cast the Bless spell once using this benefit, targeting only creatures carrying a wicker charm you've woven, and you can't do so again until you finish a Long Rest. You can also cast the spell using any spell slots you have. Your spellcasting ability for casting the spell in this way is Intelligence, Wisdom, or Charisma (choose the ability when you select this feat).

description | Twisted Hex | As a Magic action, you can weave spite into a wicker charm you touch. Choose an ability. When another creature is carrying the charm, or while this charm is within an area that the creature considers home, it has Disadvantage on ability checks and attack rolls using the chosen ability. When the creature fails one of these rolls, it can make a Charisma saving throw, destroying the charm on a success (DC = 8 plus your Proficiency Bonus and the ability modifier you chose for Old Blessing). You can only have one Twisted Hex charm at a time. If you make a new one, the previous one loses its magic.

title: Chef
contents: property | Type/Prerequisites | General Feat (Lvl 4)
rule
text | You gain the following benefits.
description | Ability Score Increase | Increase your Constitution or Wisdom by 1, to a maximum of 20.
description | Cook's Utensils | You gain proficiency with Cook's Utensils if you don't already have it.

description | Replenishing Meal | As part of a Short Rest, you can cook special food if you have ingredients and Cook's Utensils on hand. You can prepare enough of this food for a number of creatures equal to 4 plus your Proficiency Bonus. At the end of the Short Rest, any creature who eats the food and spends one or more Hit Dice to regain Hit Points regains an extra 1d8 Hit Points.

description | Bolstering Treats | With 1 hour of work or when you finish a Long Rest, you can cook a number of treats equal to your Proficiency Bonus if you have ingredients and Cook's Utensils on hand. These special treats last 8 hours after being made. A creature can use a Bonus Action to eat one of those treats to gain a number of Temporary Hit Points equal to your Proficiency Bonus.

title: Child of the Sun
contents: property | Type/Prerequisites | Origin Feat (—)
rule
text | You gain the following benefits.
description | Eyes of Eirdu | You and allies within 10 feet of you have Advantage on saving throws made to avoid or end the Blinded condition.

description | Faerie Fire | You learn the Faerie Fire spell. Intelligence, Wisdom, or Charisma is your spellcasting ability for this spell (choose when you select this feat). You can cast it once without a spell slot, and you regain the ability to cast it in that way when you finish a Long Rest. You can also cast the spell using any spell slots you have.
text | When you cast Faerie Fire without a spell slot using this benefit, taking damage can't break your Concentration on the spell.

title: Close Combat Artillerist
contents: property | Type/Prerequisites | Fighting Style Feat (Fighting Style)
rule
text | You gain the following benefits.
description | Firing in Melee | Being within 5 feet of an enemy doesn't impose Disadvantage on your attack rolls with Ranged weapons.
description | Point Blank | When you hit a creature that is within 5 feet of you with a ranged attack, you gain a +2 bonus to the damage roll.

title: Cloying Mists
contents: property | Type/Prerequisites | General Feat (Lvl 4)
rule
text | You gain the following benefits.
description | Ability Score Increase | Increase your Intelligence, Wisdom, or Charisma by 1, to a maximum of 20.

description | Arise, Fog | You always have the Fog Cloud spell prepared. You can cast it without a spell slot, and you must finish a Long Rest before you can cast it in this way again. You can also cast it using spell slots you have of the appropriate level. Your spellcasting ability for the spell is the ability increased by this feat.

description | Grasping Mist | Whenever you cast Fog Cloud, nonmagical flames in the spell's Sphere are extinguished, and creatures other than you and your allies have their Speed reduced by 5 feet while in the spell's Sphere.

title: Cohort of Chaos
contents: property | Type/Prerequisites | Lvl 4, Scion of the Outer Planes
rule
text | You can channel cosmic forces of chaos to gain these benefits:
bullet | Ability Score Increase. Increase one ability score of your choice by 1, to a maximum of 20.

bullet | Chaotic Flare. When you roll a 1 or a 20 on an attack roll or a saving throw, the magic of chaos flows through you. Roll a d4 and consult the Chaotic Flares table to determine what happens. A flare lasts until the end of your next turn, and a new flare can't occur until after the first flare ends.
text | Chaotic Flares
text | d4｜Flare
text | 1｜Battle Fury. A creature of your choice that you can see is filled with reckless fury. It has advantage on attack rolls and disadvantage on ability checks.

text | 2｜Disruption Field. Waves of energy ripple around you. Every creature that starts its turn within 5 feet of you, or that moves into that area for the first time on a turn, takes 1d8 force damage.

text | 3｜Unbound. When you move, you can use some or all of your walking speed to teleport yourself once, along with any equipment you're wearing or carrying, up to the distance used to an unoccupied space that you can see.
text | 4｜Wailing Winds. Winds swirl in a 15-foot-radius sphere centered on you. You and any other creatures in that area have disadvantage on Wisdom saving throws.

title: Cold Caster
contents: property | Type/Prerequisites | General Feat (Lvl 4)
rule
text | You gain the following benefits.
description | Ability Score Increase | Increase your Intelligence, Wisdom, or Charisma by 1, to a maximum of 20.

description | Cantrip | You learn the Ray of Frost cantrip. If you already know it, you learn a different Wizard cantrip of your choice. The spell's spellcasting ability is the ability increased by this feat.

description | Frostbite | Once per turn when you hit a creature with an attack roll and deal Cold damage, you can temporarily negate the creature's defenses. The creature subtracts 1d4 from the next saving throw it makes before the end of your next turn.

title: Confidence
contents: property | Type/Prerequisites | Common Virtues Feat (—)
rule
text | Overcoming difficulties has reinforced your spirit and renewed your faith in a brighter future.
bullet | Increase your Intelligence, Wisdom, or Charisma by 1, to a maximum of 20.
bullet | You gain proficiency in saving throws using Intelligence, Wisdom, or Charisma (your choice).

title: Convincing Inquisitor
contents: property | Type/Prerequisites | Origin Feat (—)
rule
text | You know how to spot a liar, and you can get people to see your way, whether through charm, reasonable arguments, or force of will. You gain the following benefits.

description | Charming Presence | When you take the Influence action, you don't have Disadvantage on checks to influence Hostile creatures, and you have Advantage on checks to influence Indifferent creatures.
description | Multiple Paths | You can use any ability modifier when you take the Influence action to make an Intimidation or  Persuasion check.

description | Zealous Insight | When you succeed on a Search action's Wisdom (Insight) check to detect lies or evasive answers, you have Advantage on Initiative rolls involving combat against that creature for 1 hour.

title: Craft
contents: property | Type/Prerequisites | Beyond 10th Level Feat (—)
rule
text | You gain a craft slot and a craft of your choice from the Scholar's Crafts list (see Crafts).

title: Crafter
contents: property | Type/Prerequisites | Origin Feat (—)
rule
text | You gain the following benefits.
description | Tool Proficiency | You gain proficiency with three different Artisan's Tools of your choice from the Fast Crafting table.
description | Discount | Whenever you buy a nonmagical item, you receive a 20 percent discount on it.

description | Fast Crafting | When you finish a Long Rest, you can craft one piece of gear from the Fast Crafting table, provided you have the Artisan's Tools associated with that item and have proficiency with those tools. The item lasts until you finish another Long Rest, at which point the item falls apart.
text | Fast Crafting
text | Artisan's Tools｜Crafted Gear
text | Carpenter's Tools｜Ladder, Torch
text | Leatherworker's Tools｜Crossbow Bolt Case, Map or Scroll Case, Pouch
text | Mason's Tools｜Block and Tackle
text | Potter's Tools｜Jug, Lamp
text | Smith's Tools｜Ball Bearings, Bucket, Caltrops, Grappling Hook, Iron Pot
text | Tinker's Tools｜Bell, Shovel, Tinderbox
text | Weaver's Tools｜Basket, Rope, Net, Tent
text | Woodcarver's Tools｜Club, Greatclub, Quarterstaff

title: Crimson Ritualist
contents: property | Type/Prerequisites | Origin Feat (—)
rule
text | Your study of blood magic and life force has borne fruit, granting you the following benefits.
description | Blood Lash | You learn the Blood Bolt cantrip. Your spellcasting ability for it is Intelligence, Wisdom, or Charisma (choose the ability when you select this feat).

description | Bottled Life | When you finish a Long Rest, you can perform a 1-minute ritual to transfer some of your life essence into a vessel for later use. Expend a number of Hit Point Dice up to your Proficiency Bonus, and bleed into a vial of water or other liquid. As a Bonus Action, a creature can drink the vial to roll the Hit Dice you expended. The creature regains a number of Hit Points equal to the total rolled plus its Constitution modifier. The vial remains potent until you finish a Long Rest.

title: Crossbow Expert
contents: property | Type/Prerequisites | General Feat (Lvl 4, Dex 13+)
rule
text | You gain the following benefits.
description | Ability Score Increase | Increase your Dexterity score by 1, to a maximum of 20.

description | Ignore Loading | You ignore the Loading property of the Hand Crossbow, Heavy Crossbow, and Light Crossbow (all called crossbows elsewhere in this feat). If you're holding one of them, you can load a piece of ammunition into it even if you lack a free hand.
description | Firing in Melee | Being within 5 feet of an enemy doesn't impose Disadvantage on your attack rolls with crossbows.

description | Dual Wielding | When you make the extra attack of the Light property, you can add your ability modifier to the damage of the extra attack if that attack is with a crossbow that has the Light property and you aren't already adding that modifier to the damage.

title: Cruel
contents: rule

text | The challenges and struggles you've faced throughout your life have led you to delight in inflicting pain and anguish upon others. You gain a number of cruelty dice equal to your proficiency bonus. Your cruelty dice are d6s. You can roll only one cruelty die per turn, and a cruelty die is spent when you roll it.
text | You can roll a cruelty die under any of the following circumstances, with the indicated result:
bullet | When you deal damage to a creature, spend one cruelty die to deal extra damage to the creature equal to the roll.
bullet | When you score a critical hit, spend one cruelty die to gain temporary hit points equal to the roll.
bullet | When you make a Charisma (Intimidation) check, spend one cruelty die and add the roll to your check.
text | You regain all spent cruelty dice when you finish a long rest.

title: Crusher
contents: property | Type/Prerequisites | General Feat (Lvl 4)
rule
text | You gain the following benefits.
description | Ability Score Increase | Increase your Strength or Constitution by 1, to a maximum of 20.

description | Push | Once per turn, when you hit a creature with an attack that deals Bludgeoning damage, you can move it 5 feet to an unoccupied space if the target is no more than one size larger than you.
description | Enhanced Critical | When you score a Critical Hit that deals Bludgeoning damage to a creature, attack rolls against that creature have Advantage until the start of your next turn.

title: Cult Initiate
contents: property | Type/Prerequisites | Origin Feat (—)
rule
text | Your cult instructs you in eldritch rites, granting you the following benefits.

description | Two Cantrips | You learn two cantrips of your choice from the Warlock spell list. Intelligence, Wisdom, or Charisma is your spellcasting ability for this feat's spells (choose the ability when you select this feat).

description | Level 1 Spell | Choose a level 1 spell from the Warlock spell list. You always have that spell prepared. You can cast it once without a spell slot, and you regain the ability to cast it in that way when you finish a Short or Long Rest. You can also cast the spell using any spell slots you have.
description | Spell Change | Whenever you gain a new level, you can replace one of the spells you chose for this feat with a different spell of the same level from the Warlock spell list.

title: Cult of the Dragon Initiate
contents: property | Type/Prerequisites | Origin Feat (—)
rule
text | You gain the following benefits.

description | Dragon's Tongue | You know Draconic. If you already know Draconic when you select this feat, you instead learn one language of your choice from the language tables in the Player's Handbook or Chapter 2 of this book.

description | Dragon's Terror | You can take a Magic action to instill terror in a creature you can see within 30 feet of yourself. The target must succeed on a Wisdom saving throw (DC 8 plus your Wisdom modifier and Proficiency Bonus) or have the Frightened condition until the end of your next turn. If the target succeeds on the save or when the effect ends for a target, the target is immune to this effect for 24 hours.

description | Inspired by Fear | When you cause a creature to have the Frightened condition and you are the source of its fear, you can gain Heroic Inspiration if you lack it. Once you use this benefit, you can't use it again until you finish a Short or Long Rest.

title: Dark for Dark Business
contents: property | Type/Prerequisites | Virtues of the Dwarves Feat (Dwarven)
rule

text | Your kind is not troubled by mere darkness in itself, as most members of other cultures are. On the contrary, you have grown to like the dark better than the light, finding yourself at ease in its cold embrace.
bullet | Increase your Wisdom score by 1, to a maximum of 20.
bullet | Dim light doesn't impose disadvantage on your Wisdom (Perception) checks relying on sight.

bullet | When you are in dim light, in darkness, or underground, and you make an ability check, attack roll, or saving throw, you can do so with advantage. You can use this virtue a number of times equal to your proficiency bonus, and you regain all expended uses when you finish a long rest.

title: Deadly Archery
contents: property | Type/Prerequisites | Virtues of the Elves Feat (Elven)
rule
text | Elves possess a natural talent for hitting the mark when using their bows. You have honed this talent almost to perfection, and your arrows find their target with uncanny precision.
bullet | Increase your Dexterity score by 1, to a maximum of 20.

bullet | As a bonus action, you can give yourself advantage on your next ranged weapon attack roll on the current turn. You can use this bonus action only if you haven't moved during this turn, and after you use the bonus action, your speed is 0 until the end of the current turn.

title: Death Defier
contents: property | Type/Prerequisites | General Feat (Lvl 4)
rule
text | When the hand of death reaches for your soul, you have found a way to reject its call. You gain the following benefits.
description | Ability Score Increase | Increase your Constitution score by 1, to a maximum of 20.
description | Cling to Life | You add your Constitution modifier (minimum of 1) to Death Saving Throws.

description | Enduring Vitality | If an effect would kill you outright without Death Saving Throws or would reduce you directly to 0 Hit Points without dealing damage, you are instead reduced to a number of Hit Points equal to your level. You can't use this benefit again until you finish a Long Rest.

title: Deathbound
contents: property | Type/Prerequisites | Origin Feat (—)
rule

text | You have seen death many times, up close and personal, and sometimes at your own hand. These experiences have affected you deeply, teaching you about life and death. You gain the following benefits.
description | One Last Breath | If you have two Death Saving Throw failures, you have Advantage on Death Saving Throws until you are no longer at 0 Hit Points.
description | Recuperation | When you spend a Hit Point Die during a Short Rest to recover Hit Points, you can roll the Hit Point Die twice and use the higher roll.

title: Defense
contents: property | Type/Prerequisites | Fighting Style Feat (Fighting Style)
rule
text | While you're wearing Light, Medium, or Heavy armor, you gain a +1 bonus to Armor Class.

title: Defensive Duelist
contents: property | Type/Prerequisites | General Feat (Lvl 4, Dex 13+)
rule
text | You gain the following benefits.
description | Ability Score Increase | Increase your Dexterity score by 1, to a maximum of 20.

description | Parry | If you're holding a Finesse weapon and another creature hits you with a melee attack, you can take a Reaction to add your Proficiency Bonus to your Armor Class, potentially causing the attack to miss you. You gain this bonus to your AC against melee attacks until the start of your next turn.

title: Defiance
contents: property | Type/Prerequisites | Virtues of the Men of Bree Feat (Men of Bree)
rule

text | Many realms have risen and fallen in the Lone-lands of Eriador since the Elder Days. Only ruins remain to mark their boundaries. Yet, the Bree-men have endured, revealing an unexpected reserve of strength and vigour in the face of adversity.
bullet | Increase your Constitution score by 1, to a maximum of 20.
bullet | As a bonus action, you can spend one Hit Die to heal yourself.

title: Delicious Pain
contents: property | Type/Prerequisites | General Feat (Lvl 4)
rule
text | You gain the following benefits.
description | Ability Score Increase | Increase one ability score of your choice by 1, to a maximum of 20.

description | Toughened Flesh | Immediately after you take Bludgeoning, Piercing, or Slashing damage, you can take a Reaction to gain Resistance to Bludgeoning, Piercing, and Slashing damage until the start of your next turn. Once you use this benefit, you can't use it again until you finish a Short or Long Rest.

title: Desperate Courage
contents: property | Type/Prerequisites | Virtues of the Men of Bree Feat (Men of Bree/Bree-Hobbit)
rule

text | You have lived all your life on the edge of the Wild, but the worst news that has come to Bree in your time concerned only brigands or packs of hungry wolves. You don't know yet what the likes of you can do against the Shadow in the East, but you are sure you will stand against it, no matter the cost.
bullet | Increase one ability score of your choice by 1, to a maximum of 20.
bullet | Whenever you gain Shadow points, you also gain inspiration.

title: Divinely Favored
contents: property | Type/Prerequisites | Lvl 4, Dragonlance
rule
text | A god chose you to carry a spark of their power.

text | You learn one cantrip of your choice from the cleric spell list and one 1st-level spell based on the alignment of your character, as specified in the Alignment Spells table below. You also learn the augury spell.
text | Alignment Spells
text | Alignment｜1st-Level Spell
text | Evil｜Choose one 1st-level warlock spell
text | Good｜Choose one 1st-level cleric spell
text | Neutral｜Choose one 1st-level druid spell

text | You can cast the chosen 1st-level spell and the augury spell without a spell slot, and you must finish a long rest before you can cast either of these spells in this way again. You can also cast these spells using spell slots you have of the appropriate level.
text | Your spellcasting ability for this feat's spells is Intelligence, Wisdom, or Charisma (choose when you select this feat).
text | In addition, you can use a holy symbol as a spellcasting focus for any spell you cast that uses the spellcasting ability you choose when you select this feat.

title: Dour-Handed
contents: property | Type/Prerequisites | Common Virtues Feat (—)
rule
text | Your blows have become more forceful and your aim steadier, allowing you to do greater harm.
bullet | Increase your Strength score by 1, to a maximum of 20.
bullet | You gain a +1 bonus to weapon damage rolls using Strength.

title: Dragon Fear
contents: property | Type/Prerequisites | Dragonborn
rule
text | When angered, you radiate menace. You gain the following benefits:
bullet | Increase your Strength, Constitution, or Charisma by 1, to a maximum of 20.

bullet | Instead of exhaling destructive energy, you can expend a use of your Breath Weapon trait to roar, forcing each creature of your choice within 30 feet of you to make a Wisdom saving throw (DC 8 + your proficiency bonus + your Charisma modifier). A target automatically succeeds on the save if it can't hear or see you. On a failed save, a target becomes frightened of you for 1 minute. If the frightened target takes any damage, it can repeat the saving throw, ending the effect on itself on a success.

title: Dragon Hide
contents: property | Type/Prerequisites | Dragonborn
rule
text | You manifest scales and claws reminiscent of your draconic ancestors. You gain the following benefits:
bullet | Increase your Strength, Constitution, or Charisma by 1, to a maximum of 20.
bullet | Your scales harden. While you aren't wearing armor, you can calculate your AC as 13 + your Dexterity modifier. You can use a shield and still gain this benefit.

bullet | You grow retractable claws from the tips of your fingers. Extending or retracting the claws requires no action. The claws are natural weapons, which you can use to make unarmed strikes. If you hit with them, you deal slashing damage equal to 1d4 + your Strength modifier, instead of the normal bludgeoning damage for an unarmed strike.

title: Dragon-Slayer
contents: property | Type/Prerequisites | Virtues of the Bardings Feat (Barding)
rule

text | The legend of Bard the Bowman has inspired many young men and women from Dale, so much that they long to prove their worth with the killing of a great monster. As many more before you, you have long pondered on the ways to deal with creatures of size, hoping one day to gain renown defeating them.
bullet | Increase your Strength or Dexterity by 1, to a maximum of 20.
bullet | You have advantage on attack rolls against Large or larger creatures.

title: Dragonscarred
contents: property | Type/Prerequisites | General Feat (Lvl 4, Cult of the Dragon Initiate)
rule
text | You gain the following benefits.
description | Ability Score Increase | Increase your Constitution or Charisma by 1, to a maximum of 20.
description | Damage Resistance | When you gain this feat, choose Acid, Cold, Fire, Lightning, or Poison. You have Resistance to the chosen damage type.

description | Fearsome Power | When you deal damage to a creature as part of the Attack or Magic action on your turn, you can use the Dragon's Terror benefit of the Cult of the Dragon Initiate feat as a Bonus Action this turn.

title: Dread Speech
contents: property | Type/Prerequisites | Origin Feat (—)
rule
text | You have articulated a few words of forbidden truth that resonate with—and fray—the fabric of creation, granting you the following benefits.

description | Eldritch Word | As a Bonus Action, you can speak a magical word aloud to force a creature within 60 feet of yourself to make a Wisdom saving throw (DC = 8 plus your Proficiency Bonus and Intelligence, Wisdom, or Charisma modifier; choose the ability when you select this feat). On a failed save, the target has the Frightened condition until the start of your next turn. You can use this benefit safely once, and you regain the ability to do so when you finish a Short or Long Rest. You can use it again before resting, but you take 1d6 Necrotic damage, which can't be reduced or prevented in any way.

description | Shred Fate | Your dread secrets can unravel the destiny of your foes. You always have the Bane spell prepared, and your spellcasting ability for it is the ability you chose for Eldritch Word. You can cast it once without Material components or a spell slot, and you regain the ability to cast it in that way when you finish a Long Rest. You can also cast the spell using any spell slots you have.

title: Drow High Magic
contents: property | Type/Prerequisites | Elf (drow)
rule

text | You learn more of the magic typical of dark elves. You learn the detect magic spell and can cast it at will, without expending a spell slot. You also learn levitate and dispel magic, each of which you can cast once without expending a spell slot. You regain the ability to cast those two spells in this way when you finish a long rest. Charisma is your spellcasting ability for all three spells.

title: Druidic Warrior
contents: property | Type/Prerequisites | Fighting Style Replacement (Ranger) (Special)
rule

text | You learn two Druid cantrips of your choice. Guidance and Starry Wisp are recommended. The chosen cantrips count as Ranger spells for you, and Wisdom is your spellcasting ability for them. Whenever you gain a Ranger level, you can replace one of these cantrips with another Druid cantrip.

title: Dual Shot
contents: property | Type/Prerequisites | Fighting Style Feat (Fighting Style)
rule
text | You gain the following benefits.

description | Dual Shot | When you take the Attack action on your turn and attack with a bow or crossbow, you can make one extra attack as part of the same action against a creature that is within 10 feet of the original target that is within the weapon's range. If you do so, both attacks are made with Disadvantage.

title: Dual Wielder
contents: property | Type/Prerequisites | General Feat (Lvl 4 + Str 13+/Dex 13+)
rule
text | You gain the following benefits.
description | Ability Score Increase | Increase your Strength or Dexterity by 1, to a maximum of 20.

description | Enhanced Dual Wielding | When you take the Attack action on your turn and attack with a weapon that has the Light property, you can make one extra attack as a Bonus Action later on the same turn with a different weapon, which must be a Melee weapon that lacks the Two-Handed property. You don't add your ability modifier to the extra attack's damage unless that modifier is negative.
description | Quick Draw | You can draw or stow two weapons that lack the Two-Handed property when you would normally be able to draw or stow only one.

title: Dueling
contents: property | Type/Prerequisites | Fighting Style Feat (Fighting Style)
rule
text | When you're holding a Melee weapon in one hand and no other weapons, you gain a +2 bonus to damage rolls with that weapon.

title: Dungeon Delver
contents: rule
text | Alert to the hidden traps and secret doors found in many dungeons, you gain the following benefits:
bullet | You have advantage on Wisdom (Perception) and Intelligence (Investigation) checks made to detect the presence of secret doors.
bullet | You have advantage on saving throws made to avoid or resist traps.
bullet | You have resistance to the damage dealt by traps.
bullet | Traveling at a fast pace doesn't impose the normal -5 penalty on your passive Wisdom (Perception) score.

title: Durable
contents: property | Type/Prerequisites | General Feat (Lvl 4)
rule
text | You gain the following benefits.
description | Ability Score Increase | Increase your Constitution score by 1, to a maximum of 20.
description | Defy Death | You have Advantage on Death Saving Throws.
description | Speedy Recovery | As a Bonus Action, you can expend one of your Hit Point Dice, roll the die, and regain a number of Hit Points equal to the roll.

title: Durin's Way
contents: property | Type/Prerequisites | Virtues of the Dwarves Feat (Dwarven)
rule

text | The Dwarves have fought many wars in deep places beneath the earth. You have been taught how to best defend yourself while fighting underground. As a result, you know how to exploit corners, darkness and other natural obstacles to your advantage.
bullet | Increase your Dexterity score by 1, to a maximum of 20.
bullet | When you have half or three-quarters cover, you gain an additional +1 bonus to AC and Dexterity saving throws.
bullet | Other creatures do not gain advantage on attack rolls against you as a result of being unseen by you.

title: Dwarf-Friend
contents: property | Type/Prerequisites | Virtues of the Bardings Feat (Barding)
rule
text | The days of the Dragon have forged a strong alliance between the Bardings and the Dwarves of Erebor.
bullet | Increase your Charisma score by 1, to a maximum of 20.
bullet | Your proficiency bonus is doubled for any Charisma (Persuasion) check you make. In addition, you have advantage on Charisma (Persuasion) checks made when interacting with Dwarves.
bullet | Increase the Fellowship rating of your Company by 1.

bullet | When you are within 5 feet of a friendly Dwarf and at least one of you isn't incapacitated, and a creature you or the Dwarf can see attacks you or the Dwarf, you can spend a Fellowship point to impose disadvantage on the attack roll.

title: Dwarven Fortitude
contents: property | Type/Prerequisites | Dwarf
rule
text | You have the blood of dwarf heroes flowing through your veins. You gain the following benefits:
bullet | Increase your Constitution score by 1, to a maximum of 20.

bullet | Whenever you take the Dodge action in combat, you can spend one Hit Die to heal yourself. Roll the die, add your Constitution modifier, and regain a number of hit points equal to the total (minimum of 1).

title: Elbereth Gilthoniel!
contents: property | Type/Prerequisites | Virtues of the Elves Feat (Elven)
rule

text | The name of Elbereth, the Queen of the Stars, is revered by all the Eldar still dwelling in Middle-earth. You have resorted to calling on her name in moments of great need, asking the Kindler to bestow her grace upon you.
bullet | Increase your Intelligence, Wisdom, or Charisma by 1, to a maximum of 20.

bullet | When you are not miserable and make an ability check, attack roll, or saving throw, you can do so with advantage. You can use this virtue a number of times equal to your proficiency bonus, and you regain all expended uses when you finish a long rest.

title: Eldritch Adept
contents: property | Type/Prerequisites | Spellcasting
rule

text | Studying occult lore, you learn one Eldritch Invocation option of your choice from the warlock class. Your spellcasting ability for the invocation is Intelligence, Wisdom, or Charisma (choose when you select this feat). If the invocation has a prerequisite of any kind, you can choose that invocation only if you're a warlock who meets the prerequisite.
text | Whenever you gain a level, you can replace the invocation with another one from the warlock class.

title: Elemental Adept
contents: property | Type/Prerequisites | General Feat (Lvl 4, Spellcasting)
rule
text | You gain the following benefits.
description | Ability Score Increase | Increase your Intelligence, Wisdom, or Charisma by 1, to a maximum of 20.

description | Energy Mastery | Choose one of the following damage types: Acid, Cold, Fire, Lightning, or Thunder. Spells you cast ignore Resistance to damage of the chosen type. In addition, when you roll damage for a spell you cast that deals damage of that type, you can treat any 1 on a damage die as a 2.
description | Repeatable | You can take this feat more than once, but you must choose a different damage type each time for Energy Mastery.

title: Elven Accuracy
contents: property | Type/Prerequisites | Elf/Half-Elf
rule

text | The accuracy of elves is legendary, especially that of elf archers and spellcasters. You have uncanny aim with attacks that rely on precision rather than brute force. You gain the following benefits:
bullet | Increase your Dexterity, Intelligence, Wisdom, or Charisma by 1, to a maximum of 20.
bullet | Whenever you have advantage on an attack roll using Dexterity, Intelligence, Wisdom, or Charisma, you can reroll one of the dice once.

title: Elvish Spirit
contents: property | Type/Prerequisites | Virtues of the Elves Feat (Elven)
rule
text | The spirit of Elves is so strong that their bodies recover swiftly from many ills and injuries. You can recover from your exertions while awake, finding repose in the secret way of your folk.
bullet | Increase your Constitution score by 1, to a maximum of 20.

bullet | If you meditate for at least 4 hours, you can reduce your exhaustion level by 1 and regain all lost hit points. You cannot use this virtue if you've had a long rest within the last 24 hours, or if you have 0 hit points. Once you use this virtue, you cannot use it again until you finish a long rest.

title: Ember of the Fire Giant
contents: property | Type/Prerequisites | Lvl 4, Strike of the Giants
rule
text | You've manifested the fiery combat emblematic of fire giants, granting you the following benefits:
bullet | Ability Score Increase. Increase your Strength, Constitution, or Wisdom by 1, to a maximum of 20.
bullet | Born of Flame. You have resistance to fire damage.

bullet | Searing Ignition. When you take the Attack action on your turn, you can replace a single attack with a magical burst of flame. Each creature of your choice in a 15-foot-radius sphere centered on you must make a Dexterity saving throw (DC equals 8 + your proficiency bonus + the modifier of the ability increased by this feat). On a failed save, a creature takes fire damage equal to 1d8 + your proficiency bonus, and it has the blinded condition until the start of your next turn. On a successful save, the creature takes half as much damage only. You can use your Searing Ignition a number of times equal to your proficiency bonus (but no more than once per turn), and you regain all expended uses when you finish a long rest.

title: Emerald Enclave Fledgling
contents: property | Type/Prerequisites | Origin Feat (—)
rule
text | You gain the following benefits.

description | Speak with Animals | You always have the Speak with Animals spell prepared and can cast it with any spell slots you have. Intelligence, Wisdom, or Charisma is your spellcasting ability for this spell (choose when you select this feat). When you cast this spell as a Ritual, its duration is 8 hours.

description | Tag Team | When you take the Help action, you can switch places with a willing ally within 5 feet of yourself as part of that same action. This movement doesn't provoke Opportunity Attacks. You can't use this benefit if the ally has the Incapacitated condition.

title: Enclave Magic
contents: property | Type/Prerequisites | General Feat (Lvl 4, Emerald Enclave Fledgling)
rule
text | You gain the following benefits.
description | Ability Score Increase | Increase your Intelligence, Wisdom, or Charisma by 1, to a maximum of 20.
description | Friend to Animals | You have Advantage on ability checks when taking the Influence action with Beasts.

description | Two Hearts, One Mind | You always have the Beast Sense spell prepared. You can cast it once without a spell slot, and you regain the ability to cast it in that way when you finish a Long Rest. When you cast it without a spell slot using this feature, it doesn't require Concentration. You can also cast the spell using any spell slots you have of the appropriate level. The spell's spellcasting ability is the ability increased by this feat.

title: Endurance of the Ranger
contents: property | Type/Prerequisites | Virtues of the Rangers of the North Feat (Ranger of the North)
rule

text | They say that a Ranger with a clear trail to follow can never be weary. You hope the saying will prove true, as it will be your fate to often travel in haste, to seek distant lands on urgent errands or to relentlessly hunt your enemies.
bullet | Increase your Constitution score by 1, to a maximum of 20.
bullet | You have advantage on Constitution saving throws.

title: Expanded Grip
contents: property | Type/Prerequisites | General Feat (Lvl 4, Str 13+)
rule
text | Your natural ability with larger weapons lets you use them in a way others can't. You gain the following benefits.
description | Ability Score Increase | Increase your Strength, Dexterity, or Constitution by 1, to a maximum of 20.
description | One-Handed Grip | When you use a Versatile weapon with one hand, the weapon deals the damage in parentheses when used to make a melee attack.

description | Zealous Grasp | If an effect forces you to drop what you are holding, you can make a DC 15 Strength saving throw to maintain your grip. When you are subjected to an effect that allows you to make a saving throw to maintain your grip, you have Advantage on the saving throw.

title: Fade Away
contents: property | Type/Prerequisites | Gnome
rule
text | Your people are clever, with a knack for illusion magic. You have learned a magical trick for fading away when you suffer harm. You gain the following benefits:
bullet | Increase your Dexterity or Intelligence by 1, to a maximum of 20.

bullet | Immediately after you take damage, you can use a reaction to magically become invisible until the end of your next turn or until you attack, deal damage, or force someone to make a saving throw. Once you use this ability, you can't do so again until you finish a short or long rest.

title: Fairy Trickster
contents: property | Type/Prerequisites | General Feat (Lvl 4)
rule
text | You gain the following benefits.
description | Ability Score Increase | Increase your Dexterity or Charisma by 1, to a maximum of 20.
description | Faerie Trod Trotter | When you take the Disengage action on your turn, Difficult Terrain doesn't cost you extra movement for the rest of that turn.

description | Flustering Strike | When you hit a creature with an attack roll, you can attempt to fluster the target. The target must succeed on a Wisdom saving throw (DC 8 plus the ability modifier of the score increased by this feat and your Proficiency Bonus) or have Disadvantage on saving throws until the end of your next turn.
text | You can use this benefit a number of times equal to your Proficiency Bonus, and you regain all expended uses when you finish a Long Rest.
