text | You've learned how to exert your will on your spells to alter how they function:

bullet | You learn two Metamagic options of your choice from the sorcerer class. You can use only one Metamagic option on a spell when you cast it, unless the option says otherwise. Whenever you reach a level that grants the Ability Score Improvement feature, you can replace one of these Metamagic options with another one from the sorcerer class.

bullet | You gain 2 sorcery points to spend on Metamagic (these points are added to any sorcery points you have from another source but can be used only on Metamagic). You regain all spent sorcery points when you finish a long rest.

title: Mobile Combatant
contents: property | Type/Prerequisites | Fighting Style Feat (Fighting Style)
rule
text | You gain the following benefits.
description | Slippery | When you take the Attack action, your Speed increases by 10 feet and Opportunity Attacks have Disadvantage against you until the end of your turn.

title: Moderately Armored
contents: property | Type/Prerequisites | General Feat (Lvl 4, L. Armor Trai.)
rule
text | You gain the following benefits.
description | Ability Score Increase | Increase your Strength or Dexterity by 1, to a maximum of 20.
description | Armor Training | You gain training with Medium armor.

title: Mounted Combatant
contents: property | Type/Prerequisites | General Feat (Lvl 4)
rule
text | You gain the following benefits.
description | Ability Score Increase | Increase your Strength, Dexterity, or Wisdom by 1, to a maximum of 20.
description | Mounted Strike | While mounted, you have Advantage on attack rolls against any unmounted creature within 5 feet of your mount that is at least one size smaller than the mount.

description | Leap Aside | If your mount is subjected to an effect that allows it to make a Dexterity saving throw to take only half damage, it instead takes no damage if it succeeds on the saving throw and only half damage if it fails. For your mount to gain this benefit, you must be riding it, and neither of you can have the Incapacitated condition.
description | Veer | While mounted, you can force an attack that hits your mount to hit you instead if you don't have the Incapacitated condition.

title: Musician
contents: property | Type/Prerequisites | Origin Feat (—)
rule
text | You gain the following benefits.
description | Instrument Training | You gain proficiency with three Musical Instruments of your choice.

description | Encouraging Song | As you finish a Short or Long Rest, you can play a song on a Musical Instrument with which you have proficiency and give Heroic Inspiration to allies who hear the song. The number of allies you can affect in this way equals your Proficiency Bonus.

title: Mystic Conflux
contents: rule

text | You possess an intuitive understanding of the way magic ebbs and flows within enchanted items. Such items attune easily to you, and you are able to sound out their secrets. You gain the following benefits:
bullet | You can attune to up to four magic items at once.
bullet | You can cast the identify spell without expending a spell slot or material components. You must finish a long rest before you can do so again.

title: Mythal Touched
contents: property | Type/Prerequisites | General Feat (Lvl 4)
rule
description | Ability Score Increase | Increase your Intelligence, Wisdom, or Charisma by 1, to a maximum of 20.

description | Mythal Ward | If a spell attack hits you or you fail a saving throw against a spell, you can take a Reaction to roll on the Mythal-Touched Magic table to create a magical effect. If an effect requires a saving throw, the DC equals 8 plus the modifier of the ability increased by this feat and your Proficiency Bonus.
text | You can use this benefit a number of times equal to your Proficiency Bonus, and you regain all expended uses when you finish a Long Rest.
text | Mythal-Touched Magic
text | 1d20｜Effect

text | 1-2｜You and each creature within 15 feet of you make a Dexterity saving throw, taking Force damage equal to 1d8 times the level of the triggering spell on a failed save or half as much damage on a successful one.
text | 3-7｜You and the triggering spell's caster form a telepathic link for 1 hour.
text | 8-10｜Gravity is reversed in a 15-foot-radius, 60-foot-tall Cylinder centered on you for 1 minute, per the Reverse Gravity spell.
text | 11-13｜You and the triggering spell's caster each make a Constitution saving throw. On a failed save, the creature has the Stunned condition until the end of its next turn.
text | 14-17｜You gain a +2 bonus to AC for 1 minute, potentially turning the triggering spell into a miss if it was a spell attack.

text | 18-19｜Any flammable, nonmagical object within 10 feet of the triggering spell's caster that isn't being worn or carried by another creature bursts into flame, takes 1d4 Fire damage, and is burning.
text | 20｜The triggering spell dissipates with no effect, and the action, Bonus Action, or Reaction used to cast it is wasted. If that spell was cast with a spell slot, the slot isn't expended.

title: Nimble Physique
contents: property | Type/Prerequisites | General Feat (Lvl 4, Dex 13+)
rule
text | You are small and thin for your species. You have a mysterious and consistent ability to avoid danger. You gain the following benefits:
bullet | Ability Score Increase. Increase your Strength, Dexterity, or Constitution by 1, to a maximum of 20.
bullet | Dodgy. While you aren't wearing armor or wielding a Shield, you can take the Dodge action as a Bonus Action.
bullet | Slippery. While you are Grappled or Restrained, your attacks don't have Disadvantage and attacks against you don't have Advantage.

title: Nimbleness
contents: property | Type/Prerequisites | Common Virtues Feat (—)
rule
text | Your skill (or fortune?) in battle has increased with your growth in experience.
bullet | Increase your Dexterity score by 1, to a maximum of 20.
bullet | You gain a +1 bonus to AC. You lose this bonus if you are incapacitated or wielding a shield.

title: Observant
contents: property | Type/Prerequisites | General Feat (Lvl 4 + Int 13+/Wis 13+)
rule
text | You gain the following benefits.
description | Ability Score Increase | Increase your Intelligence or Wisdom by 1, to a maximum of 20.

description | Keen Observer | Choose one of the following skills: Insight, Investigation, or Perception. If you lack proficiency with the chosen skill, you gain proficiency in it, and if you already have proficiency in it, you gain Expertise in it.
description | Quick Search | You can take the Search action as a Bonus Action.

title: Opportunist
contents: property | Type/Prerequisites | Fighting Style Feat (Fighting Style)
rule
text | You gain the following benefits.
description | Exploit Weakness | Whenever you make an attack as part of a Reaction, you gain a +2 bonus to the attack and damage rolls.

title: Opportunistic Thief
contents: rule
text | You have learned the tricks of the trade of thievery, allowing you to exploit opportunities for pick-pocketing both in and out of combat. You gain the following benefits:
bullet | Increase your Dexterity score by 1, to a maximum of 20.

bullet | When a creature fails a melee attack roll against you in combat, you can make a Dexterity (Sleight of Hand) check against a DC equal to 10 + the target's Dexterity modifier. On a success, you may steal any one item that is not being held or worn by the target.

bullet | Whenever you successfully use your Sleight of Hand skill outside of combat to steal an object, you may immediately conceal it flawlessly on your person, or put another object you possess in its place.

title: Orcish Fury
contents: property | Type/Prerequisites | Half-Orc
rule
text | Your fury burns tirelessly. You gain the following benefits:
bullet | Increase your Strength or Constitution by 1, to a maximum of 20.

bullet | When you hit with an attack using a simple or martial weapon, you can roll one of the weapon's damage dice an additional time and add it as extra damage of the weapon's damage type. Once you use this ability, you can't use it again until you finish a short or long rest.
bullet | Immediately after you use your Relentless Endurance trait, you can use your reaction to make one weapon attack.

title: Order's Resilience
contents: property | Type/Prerequisites | General Feat (Lvl 4, Tyro of the Gauntlet)
rule
text | You gain the following benefits.
description | Ability Score Increase | Increase your Strength, Wisdom, or Charisma by 1, to a maximum of 20.
description | Resurge | When you have the Prone condition, you can right yourself with only 5 feet of movement.

description | Stronger Together | If you are within 5 feet of an ally that doesn't have the Incapacitated condition, you and that ally have Advantage on Strength saving throws. You can't use this benefit while you have the Incapacitated condition.

title: Outlands Envoy
contents: property | Type/Prerequisites | Lvl 4, Scion of the Outer Planes
rule
text | You have spent significant time in Sigil or the Outlands, the crossroads of the multiverse. Being steeped in converging planar energies grants you these benefits:
bullet | Ability Score Increase. Increase one ability score of your choice by 1, to a maximum of 20.

bullet | Crossroads Emissary. You learn the misty step and tongues spells. You can cast each spell once using this feat without a spell slot, and you must finish a long rest before you can cast that spell in this way again. When you cast tongues using this feat, you require no material components. You can also cast these spells using spell slots you have of the appropriate level. The spells' spellcasting ability is the one chosen when you gained the Scion of the Outer Planes feat.

title: Perfect Landing
contents: rule
text | Years of living at great heights have taught you how to fall more gracefully. You gain the following benefits:
bullet | Increase your Dexterity score by 1, to a maximum of 20.
bullet | Reduce the damage die for fall damage from a d6 to a d4.
bullet | You do not fall prone after taking falling damage.
bullet | You do not take damage for the first 30 feet of your fall.

title: Piercer
contents: property | Type/Prerequisites | General Feat (Lvl 4)
rule
text | You gain the following benefits.
description | Ability Score Increase | Increase your Strength or Dexterity by 1, to a maximum of 20.
description | Puncture | Once per turn, when you hit a creature with an attack that deals Piercing damage, you can reroll one of the attack's damage dice, and you must use the new roll.

description | Enhanced Critical | When you score a Critical Hit that deals Piercing damage to a creature, you can roll one additional damage die when determining the extra Piercing damage the target takes.

title: Planar Wanderer
contents: property | Type/Prerequisites | Lvl 4, Scion of the Outer Planes
rule
text | You can draw on the forces of the multiverse to survive cosmic extremes and to traverse its infinite realms, granting you these benefits:
bullet | Planar Adaptation. When you finish a long rest, you gain resistance to either acid, cold, or fire damage (your choice) until you finish your next long rest.

bullet | Portal Cracker. Your experience with portals allows you to open them without a portal key. As an action, you can concentrate on a portal you're aware of that is within 5 feet of yourself and make a DC 20 Intelligence (Arcana) check. On a failed check, you take 3d8 psychic damage and can't use this benefit on that portal again until you finish a long rest. On a successful check, you can force the portal open or closed for 1 hour. For that duration, the portal doesn't respond to its portal key unless a creature employing the key succeeds on a DC 20 Intelligence (Arcana) check as an action.

bullet | Portal Sense. You know the direction to the last planar portal you used while you and the portal are on the same plane of existence. Moreover, as an action, you can detect the location of any portals within 30 feet of you that aren't behind total cover. Once you detect a portal with this action, you can't use the action again until you finish a long rest.

title: Plantmender
contents: property | Type/Prerequisites | Wis 13+
rule

text | One of the basic forms of Tender magic, plantmending is often taught to seedlings of the order. Plantmending can be used either to help the forest recover from disasters or to defend against hazards that might be encountered while traveling. You gain the following benefits:

bullet | You can touch a plant or tree as an action to determine what has occurred to it and the area within its immediate vicinity within the last 24 hours. These events are revealed to you in visions that appear as though you were present as they occurred. After using this ability on a plant, you instantly become aware of its status and health, including any blights or conditions currently affecting it. You can use this ability a number of times equal to twice your Wisdom modifier (minimum of 1), regaining expended uses of it when you complete a long rest.
bullet | You learn the mend plants* and shillelagh cantrips. Wisdom is your spellcasting ability for these spells.
bullet | You can cast either the barkskin or spike growth spell once, and you must complete a long rest before you can cast either spell again. Wisdom is your spellcasting ability for these spells.

title: Poisoner
contents: property | Type/Prerequisites | General Feat (Lvl 4)
rule
text | You gain the following benefits.
description | Ability Score Increase | Increase your Dexterity or Intelligence by 1, to a maximum of 20.
description | Potent Poison | When you make a damage roll that deals Poison damage, it ignores Resistance to Poison damage.

description | Brew Poison | You gain proficiency with the Poisoner's Kit. With 1 hour of work using such a kit and expending 50 GP worth of materials, you can create a number of poison doses equal to your Proficiency Bonus. As a Bonus Action, you can apply a poison dose to a weapon or piece of ammunition. Once applied, the poison retains its potency for 1 minute or until you deal damage with the poisoned item, whichever is shorter. When a creature takes damage from the poisoned item, that creature must succeed on a Constitution saving throw (DC 8 plus the modifier of the ability increased by this feat and your Proficiency Bonus) or take 2d8 Poison damage and have the Poisoned condition until the end of your next turn.

title: Polearm Master
contents: property | Type/Prerequisites | General Feat (Lvl 4 + Str 13+/Dex 13+)
rule
text | You gain the following benefits.
description | Ability Score Increase | Increase your Dexterity or Strength by 1, to a maximum of 20.

description | Pole Strike | Immediately after you take the Attack action and attack with a Quarterstaff, a Spear, or a weapon that has the Heavy and Reach properties, you can use a Bonus Action to make a melee attack with the opposite end of the weapon. The weapon deals Bludgeoning damage, and the weapon's damage die for this attack is a d4.

description | Reactive Strike | While you're holding a Quarterstaff, a Spear, or a weapon that has the Heavy and Reach properties, you can take a Reaction to make one melee attack against a creature that enters the reach you have with that weapon.

title: Potent Dragonmark
contents: property | Type/Prerequisites | General Feat (Lvl 4, Any Dragonmark)
rule
text | You gain the following benefits.
description | Ability Score Increase | Increase the spellcasting ability score used by your Dragonmark feat by 1, to a maximum of 20.
description | Dragonmark Preparation | You always have the spells on your Spells of the Mark list (if any) prepared.

description | Dragonmark Spellcasting | You have one extra spell slot to cast the spells granted by your Dragonmark feat. The spell slot's level is half your level (round up), to a maximum of level 5. You regain the expended slot when you finish a Short or Long Rest. You can use this spell slot to cast only a spell you have prepared because of your Dragonmark feat or the Dragonmark Preparation benefit of this feat.

title: Prodigy
contents: property | Type/Prerequisites | Half-Elf/Half-Orc/Human
rule
text | You have a knack for learning new things. You gain the following benefits:
bullet | You gain one skill proficiency of your choice, one tool proficiency of your choice, and fluency in one language of your choice.

bullet | Choose one skill in which you have proficiency. You gain expertise with that skill, which means your proficiency bonus is doubled for any ability check you make with it. The skill you choose must be one that isn't already benefiting from a feature, such as Expertise, that doubles your proficiency bonus.

title: Prone Defense
contents: property | Type/Prerequisites | Fighting Style Feat (Fighting Style)
rule
text | You gain the following benefits.
description | Defensive | When you have the Prone condition, you don't have Disadvantage on attack rolls. Attack roll against you doesn't have Advantage because of the Prone condition.
description | Hop Up | When you have the Prone condition, you can right yourself with only 5 feet of movement.

title: Protection
contents: property | Type/Prerequisites | Fighting Style Feat (Fighting Style)
rule

text | When a creature you can see attacks a target other than you that is within 5 feet of you, you can take a Reaction to interpose your Shield if you're holding one. You impose Disadvantage on the triggering attack roll and all other attack rolls against the target until the start of your next turn if you remain within 5 feet of the target.

title: Prowess
contents: property | Type/Prerequisites | Common Virtues Feat (—)
rule
text | Adventuring has honed your inborn talents.
bullet | Increase one ability score of your choice by 2, or two ability scores of your choice by 1, to a maximum of 20.

title: Purple Dragon Commandant
contents: property | Type/Prerequisites | General Feat (Lvl 4, Purple Dragon Rook/Prof m. weapons)
rule
description | Ability Score Increase | Increase your Strength or Dexterity by 1, to a maximum of 20.

description | Encourage Ally | As a Bonus Action, you bolster one ally you can see within 30 feet. The ally gains Temporary Hit Points equal to 2d6 plus the modifier of the ability score increased by this feat. You can take this Bonus Action a number of times equal to your Proficiency Bonus, and you regain all uses when you finish a Long Rest.
description | Last Stand | You have Advantage on attack rolls while Bloodied.

title: Purple Dragon Rook
contents: property | Type/Prerequisites | Origin Feat (—)
rule
text | You gain the following benefits.
description | Entreat | You gain proficiency in one of the following skills: Insight, Performance, or Persuasion.

description | Rallying Cry | When you roll Initiative and don't have the Incapacitated condition, you can choose a number of creatures equal to your Proficiency Bonus that you can see within 30 feet of yourself. Those creatures gain Heroic Inspiration.
text | Once you use this benefit, you can't do so again until you finish a Long Rest.

title: Putrefy
contents: property | Type/Prerequisites | General Feat (Lvl 4)
rule
text | You gain the following benefits.
description | Ability Score Increase | Increase one ability score of your choice by 1, to a maximum of 20.

description | Necrosis | When you make a damage roll that deals Necrotic damage, you can cause one creature taking that damage to have the Poisoned condition until the start of your next turn. Once you use this benefit, you can't use it again until you finish a Short or Long Rest.

title: Rebuke
contents: property | Type/Prerequisites | General Feat (Lvl 4)
rule
text | You gain the following benefits.
description | Ability Score Increase | Increase one ability score of your choice by 1, to a maximum of 20.

description | Radiant Strike | When you make a damage roll that deals Radiant damage, you can cause one Huge or smaller creature taking the damage to have the Prone condition. Once you use this benefit, you can't use it again until you finish a Short or Long Rest.

title: Remarkable Recovery
contents: rule
text | Your body has the ability to recover quickly from terrible injuries, and is unusually receptive to healing magic. You gain the following benefits:
bullet | Increase your Constitution score by 1, to a maximum of 20.
bullet | When you are successfully stabilized while dying, you regain hit points equal to your Constitution modifier (minimum of 1).
bullet | Whenever you regain hit points as a result of a spell, potion, or class feature (but not this feat), you regain additional hit points equal to your Constitution modifier (minimum of 1).

title: Resilient
contents: property | Type/Prerequisites | General Feat (Lvl 4)
rule
text | You gain the following benefits.
description | Ability Score Increase | Choose one ability in which you lack saving throw proficiency. Increase the chosen ability score by 1, to a maximum of 20.
description | Saving Throw Proficiency | You gain saving throw proficiency with the chosen ability.

title: Resolution of the Syndicate
contents: property | Type/Prerequisites | Origin Feat (—)
rule

text | Membership in the Ebon Syndicate provides perks—one of which is the motivation that comes with the understanding that the Syndicate kills those who fail too often. You gain the following benefits.

description | Quick Strike | On your first turn after an Initiative roll, add 1d4 to the first damage roll you make. This extra damage increases to 2d4 at character level 9, and to 4d4 at character level 16.

description | Resilient | Your Hit Point maximum increases by an amount equal to your character level when you gain this feat. Whenever you gain a character level thereafter, your Hit Point maximum increases by an additional 1 Hit Point.

title: Reveling Fool
contents: property | Type/Prerequisites | Origin Feat (—)
rule
text | You joined a troupe of madcap revelers and learned to take on the guise of a fool, channeling otherworldly laughter and joviality. You gain the following benefits.

description | Fool's Mask | By donning a jovial mask and a fool's, jester's, or clown's costume, you take on a new identity that hides your own. In the guise of the fool, you can get away with minor transgressions against people in positions of authority or station.

description | Jester's Repartee | You always have the Hideous Laughter spell prepared, and your spellcasting ability for it is Intelligence, Wisdom, or Charisma (choose the ability when you select this feat). You can cast it once without Material components or a spell slot, and you regain the ability to cast it in that way when you finish a Long Rest. You can also cast the spell using any spell slots you have.

title: Revenant Blade
contents: property | Type/Prerequisites | Elf
rule
text | You are descended from a master of the double-bladed scimitar, and some of that mastery has passed on to you. You gain the following benefits:
bullet | Increase your Strength or Dexterity by 1, to a maximum of 20.
bullet | While you are holding a double-bladed scimitar with two hands, you gain a +1 bonus to Armor Class.
bullet | A double-bladed scimitar has the finesse property when you wield it.

title: Reward
contents: property | Type/Prerequisites | Beyond 10th Level Feat (—)
property | Repeatable | Repeatable: Yes
rule
text | You choose a reward (see Chapter 4).
description | Special | You may take this feature multiple times.

title: Righteous Heritor
contents: property | Type/Prerequisites | Lvl 4, Scion of the Outer Planes
rule
text | You can channel cosmic forces of good to gain these benefits:
bullet | Ability Score Increase. Increase one ability score of your choice by 1, to a maximum of 20.

bullet | Soothe Pain. When you or a creature within 30 feet of you takes damage, you can use your reaction to reduce that damage by 1d10 + your proficiency bonus. You can use this benefit a number of times equal to your proficiency bonus, and you regain all expended uses when you finish a long rest.

title: Ritual Caster
contents: property | Type/Prerequisites | General Feat (Lvl 4 + Int 13+/Wis 13+/Cha 13+)
rule
text | You gain the following benefits.
description | Ability Score Increase | Increase your Intelligence, Wisdom, or Charisma by 1, to a maximum of 20.

description | Ritual Spells | Choose a number of level 1 spells equal to your Proficiency Bonus that have the Ritual tag. You always have those spells prepared, and you can cast them with any spell slots you have. The spells' spellcasting ability is the ability increased by this feat. Whenever your Proficiency Bonus increases thereafter, you can add an additional level 1 spell with the Ritual tag to the spells always prepared with this feature.

description | Quick Ritual | With this benefit, you can cast a Ritual spell that you have prepared using its regular casting time rather than the extended time for a Ritual. Doing so doesn't require a spell slot. Once you cast the spell in this way, you can't use this benefit again until you finish a Long Rest.

title: Royalty Revealed
contents: property | Type/Prerequisites | Virtues of the Rangers of the North Feat (Ranger of the North)
rule

text | Your kin belong to a bloodline of kings, but they have learnt to hide from their enemies in the twilight years of their kingdom. Should you choose to reveal your heritage, your noble bearing will cause your allies to cast away their doubts. You reveal yourself by shouting an ancestral battle-cry, or unsheathing a weapon of high lineage, or by showing a device or coat of arms.
bullet | Increase your Strength or Charisma by 1, to a maximum of 20.

bullet | As a bonus action, you can choose to reveal your lineage. When you do so, choose a number of friendly creatures (which can include yourself) within 30 feet of you who can see or hear you, up to twice your proficiency bonus. Until the end of your next turn, each creature gains advantage on attack rolls and Charisma (Intimidation) checks, and is immune to being frightened. It also gains temporary hit points equal to your choice of your Strength or Charisma modifier (minimum 1). These temporary hit points last until they are depleted or the creature finishes a short or long rest. Once you use this virtue, you cannot use it again until you finish a short or long rest.

title: Rune Shaper
contents: property | Type/Prerequisites | Spellcasting/Rune Carver
rule
text | You've studied the magic of Giant runes, granting you the following benefits:

bullet | Comprehend Languages. You learn the comprehend languages spell. You can cast this spell without expending a spell slot, and you must finish a long rest before you can cast it in this way again. You can also cast this spell using any spell slots you have.

bullet | Rune Magic. You know a number of runes equal to half your proficiency bonus (rounded down), chosen from the Rune Spells table. Whenever you finish a long rest, you can inscribe each rune you know onto one nonmagical weapon, armor, piece of clothing, or other object you touch. You temporarily learn the 1st-level spells that correspond to the runes you inscribed, as specified on the Rune Spells table, and you know those spells until you finish a long rest, when the runes fade. While you are wearing or carrying any rune-marked object, you can cast the spells associated with those runes using any spell slots you have.

text | You can also invoke a rune inscribed on an object you are wearing or carrying and cast its associated spell without expending a spell slot or using material components. Once you cast the spell in this way, you can't do so again until you finish a long rest. Your spellcasting ability for this feat is Intelligence, Wisdom, or Charisma (choose when you select this feat).
text | Each time you gain a level, you can replace one of the runes you know with another one from the Rune Spells table.
text | Rune｜Spell
text | Cloud｜Fog cloud
text | Death｜Inflict wounds
text | Dragon｜Chromatic orb
text | Enemy｜Disguise self
text | Fire｜Burning hands
text | Friend｜Speak with animals
text | Frost｜Armor of Agathys
text | Hill｜Goodberry
text | Journey｜Longstrider
text | King｜Command
text | Mountain｜Entangle
text | Stone｜Sanctuary
text | Storm｜Thunderwave

title: Rune-Craft
contents: property | Type/Prerequisites | Craft Feat (—)
rule
text | You can now read and write runic inscriptions. You can use the runes to convey secret messages to other creatures who know them, or whom you previously instructed in their meaning.

text | At the end of a short or long rest, you can spend a craft slot and choose a magical item in the Company's possession. Make an Intelligence (Old Lore) check. The DC is 10 for a Marvellous Artefact, 15 for a Wondrous Item, or 20 for a Famous Weapon or Armour. If you succeed, you learn all there is to be discovered about the item's qualities, and whether it is cursed or not.

text | In addition, during a Fellowship Phase, you can spend a craft slot to inscribe runes of power on a non-magical item of your choice, or on two items when you reach 5th level. You must be proficient with the tools related to the substance of which the item you choose is made (smith's tools for metal, woodcarver's tools for wood, etc.). While a creature holds or wears the item, it gains a +1 bonus to saving throws. If the item is a shield or a weapon, you may attribute it a bane instead (see Chapter 7). Choose a creature type among Evil Men, Orcs, Spiders, Trolls, Undead, or Wolves. If the item has one or more rewards, their effects are doubled against bane creatures (for example, a Keen weapon scores a critical hit on a roll of 18–20 against bane creatures). The runes can only be inscribed once on each item, and you cannot regain a craft slot expended in this way until the next Fellowship Phase, when you can cancel the runes inscribed with that slot and regain the slot, if you wish.

title: Sangromantic Initiate
contents: property | Type/Prerequisites | General Feat (Lvl 4, Spellcasting)
rule
text | You have become adept enough with weaving blood magic to mitigate some of the harm of Sangromancy. You gain the following benefits.

description | Blood Magic | Choose a Sangromancy spell from any spell list of a level for which you have spell slots. You always have that spell prepared. You can cast it once without a spell slot, and you regain the ability to cast it in that way when you finish a Long Rest. You can also cast the spell using any spell slot you have.

description | Sanguine Potency | You have a pool of two d12s that you can expend instead of Hit Point Dice when you cast Sangromancy spells. Your pool regains all expended dice when you finish a Long Rest.

title: Savage Attacker
contents: property | Type/Prerequisites | Origin Feat (—)
rule
text | You've trained to deal particularly damaging strikes. Once per turn when you hit a target with a weapon, you can roll the weapon's damage dice twice and use either roll against the target.

title: Scion of the Outer Planes
contents: property | Type/Prerequisites | Planescape
rule

text | Your connection to an Outer Plane infuses you with the energy there. Choose a type of plane listed in the Planar Infusion table. Your choice gives you resistance to a damage type and the ability to cast a cantrip, as specified in the table. You can cast this cantrip without material components, and your spellcasting ability for it is Intelligence, Wisdom, or Charisma (choose when you select this feat).
text | Plane｜Damage Resistance｜Cantrip
text | Chaotic Outer Plane｜Poison｜Minor illusion
text | Evil Outer Plane｜Necrotic｜Chill touch
text | Good Outer Plane｜Radiant｜Sacred flame
text | Lawful Outer Plane｜Force｜Guidance
text | The Outlands｜Psychic｜Mage hand

title: Second Chance
contents: property | Type/Prerequisites | Halfling
rule
text | Fortune favors you when someone tries to strike you. You gain the following benefits:
bullet | Increase your Dexterity, Constitution, or Charisma by 1, to a maximum of 20.

bullet | When a creature you can see hits you with an attack roll, you can use your reaction to force that creature to reroll. Once you use this ability, you can't use it again until you roll initiative at the start of combat or until you finish a short or long rest.

title: Sentinel
contents: property | Type/Prerequisites | General Feat (Lvl 4 + Str 13+/Dex 13+)
rule
text | You gain the following benefits.
description | Ability Score Increase | Increase your Strength or Dexterity by 1, to a maximum of 20.

description | Guardian | Immediately after a creature within 5 feet of you takes the Disengage action or hits a target other than you with an attack, you can make an Opportunity Attack against that creature.
description | Halt | When you hit a creature with an Opportunity Attack, the creature's Speed becomes 0 for the rest of the current turn.

title: Shadow-Touched
contents: property | Type/Prerequisites | General Feat (Lvl 4)
rule
text | Your exposure to the Shadowfell's magic grants you the following benefits.
description | Ability Score Increase | Increase your Intelligence, Wisdom, or Charisma by 1, to a maximum of 20.

description | Shadow Magic | Choose one level 1 spell from the Illusion or Necromancy school of magic. You always have that spell and the Invisibility spell prepared. You can cast each of these spells without expending a spell slot. Once you cast either spell in this way, you can't cast that spell in this way again until you finish a Long Rest. You can also cast these spells using spell slots you have of the appropriate level. The spells' spellcasting ability is the ability increased by this feat.

title: Shadowmoor Hexer
contents: property | Type/Prerequisites | Origin Feat (—)
rule
text | You gain the following benefits.

description | Hex | You always have the Hex spell prepared. Intelligence, Wisdom, or Charisma is your spellcasting ability for this spell (choose when you select this feat). You can cast it once without a spell slot, and you regain the ability to cast it in that way when you finish a Long Rest. You can also cast the spell using any spell slots you have.

description | Curse Magic | When a creature that you've cursed with Hex hits you with an attack roll, the creature takes Psychic damage equal to your Proficiency Bonus. A creature takes this damage only once per turn.

title: Shadowsteel Adept
contents: property | Type/Prerequisites | General Feat (Lvl 4, Spellcasting)
rule
text | You have learned to tap into the dangerous but powerful art of Shadowsteel casting. You gain the following benefits.
description | Ability Score Increase | Increase your Intelligence, Wisdom, or Charisma by 1, to a maximum of 20.
description | Curse Caster | You always have Shadowsteel curses prepared, and you can cast them with any spell slots you have.
description | Shadowsteel's Bite | While holding your Shadowsteel Focus, you gain a +1 bonus to spell attack rolls and to the saving throw DCs of your spells.
description | Shadowsteel Weapon | If your Shadowsteel Focus is also a weapon, that weapon has a +1 bonus to weapon attack rolls and damage rolls.

title: Shadowsteel Master
contents: property | Type/Prerequisites | General Feat (Lvl 8, Shadowsteel Adept)
rule
text | Your mastery over Shadowsteel grows stronger. You gain the following benefits.
description | Ability Score Increase | Increase your Intelligence, Wisdom, or Charisma by 1, to a maximum of 20.

description | Necrotic Harmony | While holding your Shadowsteel Focus, when you cast a spell that targets one or more creatures and requires an attack roll or saving throw, you can choose one target you hit or who failed the saving throw to also take 1d4 Necrotic damage for each spell slot level expended to cast the spell.

description | Necrotic Weapon | If your Shadowsteel Focus is also a weapon, that weapon has a +2 bonus to weapon attack rolls and damage rolls. Additionally, once on each of your turns, when you hit a creature with a weapon attack roll using your Shadowsteel Focus, you can cause the target to take an extra 2d8 Necrotic damage.

title: Sharpshooter
contents: property | Type/Prerequisites | General Feat (Lvl 4, Dex 13+)
rule
text | You gain the following benefits.
description | Ability Score Increase | Increase your Dexterity score by 1, to a maximum of 20.
description | Bypass Cover | Your ranged attacks with weapons ignore Half Cover and Three-Quarters Cover.
description | Firing in Melee | Being within 5 feet of an enemy doesn't impose Disadvantage on your attack rolls with Ranged weapons.
description | Long Shots | Attacking at long range doesn't impose Disadvantage on your attack rolls with Ranged weapons.

title: Shield Master
contents: property | Type/Prerequisites | General Feat (Lvl 4, Shield Trai.)
rule
text | You gain the following benefits.
description | Ability Score Increase | Increase your Strength score by 1, to a maximum of 20.

description | Shield Bash | If you attack a creature within 5 feet of you as part of the Attack action and hit with a Melee weapon, you can immediately bash the target with your Shield if it's equipped, forcing the target to make a Strength saving throw (DC 8 plus your Strength modifier and Proficiency Bonus). On a failed save, you either push the target 5 feet from you or cause it to have the Prone condition (your choice). You can use this benefit only once on each of your turns.

description | Interpose Shield | If you're subjected to an effect that allows you to make a Dexterity saving throw to take only half damage, you can take a Reaction to take no damage if you succeed on the saving throw and are holding a Shield.

title: Skill Expert
contents: property | Type/Prerequisites | General Feat (Lvl 4)
rule
text | You gain the following benefits.
description | Ability Score Increase | Increase one ability score of your choice by 1, to a maximum of 20.
description | Skill Proficiency | You gain proficiency in one skill of your choice.
description | Expertise | Choose one skill in which you have proficiency but lack Expertise. You gain Expertise with that skill.

title: Skilled
contents: property | Type/Prerequisites | Origin Feat (—)
rule
text | You gain proficiency in any combination of three skills or tools of your choice.
description | Repeatable | You can take this feat more than once.

title: Skulker
contents: property | Type/Prerequisites | General Feat (Lvl 4, Dex 13+)
rule
text | You gain the following benefits.
description | Ability Score Increase | Increase your Dexterity score by 1, to a maximum of 20.
description | Blindsight | You have Blindsight with a range of 10 feet.
description | Fog of War | You exploit the distractions of battle, gaining Advantage on any Dexterity (Stealth) check you make as part of the Hide action during combat.
description | Sniper | If you make an attack roll while hidden and the roll misses, making the attack roll doesn't reveal your location.

title: Slasher
contents: property | Type/Prerequisites | General Feat (Lvl 4)
rule
text | You gain the following benefits.
description | Ability Score Increase | Increase your Strength or Dexterity by 1, to a maximum of 20.
description | Hamstring | Once per turn when you hit a creature with an attack that deals Slashing damage, you can reduce the Speed of that creature by 10 feet until the start of your next turn.
description | Enhanced Critical | When you score a Critical Hit that deals Slashing damage to a creature, it has Disadvantage on attack rolls until the start of your next turn.

title: Small Folk
contents: property | Type/Prerequisites | Virtues of the Hobbits Feat (Hobbit/Bree-Hobbit)
rule
text | Hobbits are a little people, but their resourcefulness far exceeds their stature. You have learnt how to exploit your small size to your advantage in a fight.
bullet | Increase your Dexterity score by 1, to a maximum of 20.
bullet | Medium or larger creatures cannot gain advantage on attack rolls against you while you are not incapacitated.
bullet | You don't provoke opportunity attacks from Medium or larger creatures.

title: Sneak Attack
contents: property | Type/Prerequisites | Beyond 10th Level Feat (—)
rule
text | You gain the Sneak Attack feature. The extra damage is 1d6.

text | You know how to strike subtly and exploit a foe's distraction. Once per turn, you can deal an extra 1d6 damage to one creature you hit with an attack if you have advantage on the attack roll. The attack must use a finesse or a ranged weapon.
text | You don't need advantage on the attack roll if another enemy of the target is within 5 feet of it, that enemy isn't incapacitated, and you don't have disadvantage on the attack roll.

title: Song-Craft
contents: property | Type/Prerequisites | Craft Feat (—)
rule

text | You have been trained in the art of singing, according to the tradition of the wandering minstrels. Your musical lore runs deeper than most, as you partake of the knowledge of the mighty singers of old. As an action, you can spend a craft slot and make a Charisma (Performance) check, contested by the Intelligence (Riddle) check of each creature of your choice within 30 feet of you that can hear you. A creature can choose to fail its ability check if it wishes. For each target against which you win the contest, choose one of the following options:
bullet | You can suppress any effect causing the target to be charmed or frightened until the end of your next turn. This effect ends if the target can no longer hear you.

bullet | You can make the target indifferent about creatures of your choice that it is hostile towards until the end of your next turn. The target also has disadvantage on Wisdom (Perception) checks made to perceive any creature other than you for the same duration. This effect ends if the target can no longer hear you, it is harmed, or if it witnesses any of its friends being harmed.

bullet | Until the end of your next turn, whenever the target makes an attack roll or a saving throw, the target must roll a d4 and either add the number rolled to the attack roll or saving throw (if the target is friendly to you), or subtract it (if the target is hostile). This effect ends if the target can no longer hear you.
text | When you reach 5th level, you can use a bonus action on each of your turns to extend the duration of each of these effects on all targets until the end of your next turn, up to 1 minute.

title: Soul of the Storm Giant
contents: property | Type/Prerequisites | Lvl 4, Strike of the Giants
rule
text | You've manifested the tempest magic emblematic of storm giants, granting you the following benefits:
bullet | Ability Score Increase. Increase your Strength, Wisdom, or Charisma by 1, to a maximum of 20.

bullet | Maelstrom Aura. As a bonus action, you surround yourself with an aura of magical wind and lightning that extends 10 feet from you in every direction but not through total cover. The aura lasts until the start of your next turn or until you are incapacitated. While the aura is active, you have resistance to lightning and thunder damage. In addition, attack rolls against you have disadvantage, and whenever another creature starts its turn within the aura, you can force the creature to make a Strength saving throw (DC equals 8 + your proficiency bonus + the modifier of the ability increased by this feat). On a failed save, the creature's speed is halved until the start of its next turn. You can use this bonus action a number of times equal to your proficiency bonus, and you regain all expended uses when you finish a long rest.

title: Speech of the Ancient Beasts
contents: rule

text | You have a special connection with the natural world. Great beasts regard you as their kin, and you possess the ability to speak the languages of the most powerful and mystic of their kind. You gain the following benefits:
bullet | Increase your Charisma score by 1, to a maximum of 20.
bullet | Beasts of Large size or larger have a friendly disposition toward you unless you have attacked them.
bullet | You have advantage on Charisma checks made against beasts that are of Large size or larger.

bullet | You can speak and understand Giant Eagle, Giant Elk, and Giant Owl. You can otherwise be understood by any beast of Large size or larger, whether or not they speak a language. Beasts with Intelligence scores of 4 or lower may only be able to understand simple concepts.

title: Speech-Craft
contents: property | Type/Prerequisites | Craft Feat (—)
rule
text | You are a student of Sindarin and Quenya, the Ancient Tongue, and have advantage on Charisma checks when interacting with Elves.

text | In addition, as an action you can spend a craft slot and choose a creature other than an Elf, or a Wizard, or a similarly powerful creature, within 30 feet of you who can hear you. Then, choose one of the following options:

bullet | Make a Charisma (Intimidation) check contested by the target's Charisma (Intimidation) check. A target has advantage on this check if it is Large or larger or if it cannot understand you. If you win the contest, the target is frightened of you for 1 minute. The frightened target can make a Charisma (Intimidation) check against your passive Charisma (Intimidation) score at the end of each of its turns, ending the effect on itself on a success. When you reach 5th level, you can target one additional creature, or two additional creatures at 9th level.

bullet | Make a Charisma (Intimidation) or Charisma (Persuasion) check contested by the target's Wisdom (Insight) check. A target has advantage on this check if it is immune to being charmed or if it cannot understand you. If you win the contest, you can speak a one-word command that the target must follow on its next turn, if it can and if the command is not directly harmful to it. Once the target has executed the command, it ends its turn. Some typical commands include "Fall!" (the target falls prone), "Flee!" (the target moves away from you), and "Halt!" (the target doesn't move and takes no actions), and the effect may be considered a Misdeed depending on the circumstances. When you reach 5th level, you can target one additional creature, or two additional creatures at 9th level.

title: Speedy
contents: property | Type/Prerequisites | General Feat (Lvl 4 + Dex 13+/Con 13+)
rule
text | You gain the following benefits.
description | Ability Score Increase | Increase your Dexterity or Constitution by 1, to a maximum of 20.
description | Speed Increase | Your Speed increases by 10 feet.
description | Dash over Difficult Terrain | When you take the Dash action on your turn, Difficult Terrain doesn't cost you extra movement for the rest of that turn.
description | Agile Movement | Opportunity Attacks have Disadvantage against you.

title: Spell Sniper
contents: property | Type/Prerequisites | General Feat (Lvl 4, Spellcasting)
rule
text | You gain the following benefits.
description | Ability Score Increase | Increase your Intelligence, Wisdom, or Charisma by 1, to a maximum of 20.
description | Bypass Cover | Your attack rolls for spells ignore Half Cover and Three-Quarters Cover.
description | Casting in Melee | Being within 5 feet of an enemy doesn't impose Disadvantage on your attack rolls with spells.
description | Increased Range | When you cast a spell that has a range of at least 10 feet and requires you to make an attack roll, you can increase the spell's range by 60 feet.

title: Spelldriver
contents: property | Type/Prerequisites | Lvl 11, Spellcasting
rule
text | Through intense focus, training, and dedication, you've harnessed the techniques of rapid spellcasting.

text | When you use your bonus action to cast a spell of 1st level or higher, you can also use your action to cast another spell of 1st level or higher. However, if you cast two or more spells in a single turn, only one of them can be 3rd level or higher.
section | Rules Tip: Casting Multiple Spells

text | Normally, when you cast a spell as a bonus action, you can't use your action to also cast a spell that turn unless that spell is a cantrip—see PHB p202. The Spelldriver feat removes that restriction, but the focus necessary to channel multiple spells in the same round still limits the overall power of those spells.

title: Spellfire Adept
contents: property | Type/Prerequisites | General Feat (Lvl 4, Spellfire Spark/Spellcasting/Pact Magic)
rule
text | You gain the following benefits.
description | Ability Score Increase | Increase your Intelligence, Wisdom, or Charisma by 1, to a maximum of 20.

description | Fueled Spellfire | Once per turn, when a spell you cast deals Radiant damage, you can expend up to two Hit Point Dice, roll them, and add the total rolled to one damage roll of the spell.
description | Searing Spellfire | When you make a damage roll that deals Radiant damage, it ignores Resistance to Radiant damage.

title: Spellfire Spark
contents: property | Type/Prerequisites | Origin Feat (—)
rule
text | You gain the following benefits.

description | Magic Absorption | Once per turn, when you take damage from a spell or magical effect, you reduce the total damage taken by 1d4. You can't use this benefit if you have the Incapacitated condition.

description | Spellfire Flame | You learn the Sacred Flame cantrip. Intelligence, Wisdom, or Charisma is your spellcasting ability for this spell (choose when you select this feat). You can also cast this cantrip as a Bonus Action a number of times equal to your Proficiency Bonus, and you regain all expended uses when you finish a Long Rest.

title: Squat Nimbleness
contents: property | Type/Prerequisites | Dwarf/Small Race
rule
text | You are uncommonly nimble for your race. You gain the following benefits:
bullet | Increase your Strength or Dexterity by 1, to a maximum of 20.
bullet | Increase your walking speed by 5 feet.
bullet | You gain proficiency in the Acrobatics or Athletics skill (your choice).
bullet | You have advantage on any Strength (Athletics) or Dexterity (Acrobatics) check you make to escape from being grappled.

title: Squire of Solamnia
contents: property | Type/Prerequisites | Dragonlance + Figh./Pald./Knight of Solamnia
rule
text | Your training in the ways of the Knights of Solamnia grants you these benefits:
bullet | Mount Up. Mounting or dismounting costs you only 5 feet of movement.

bullet | Precise Strike. Once per turn, when you make a weapon attack roll against a creature, you can cause the attack roll to have advantage. If the attack hits, you roll a d8 and add the number rolled as a bonus to the attack's damage roll. You can use this benefit a number of times equal to your proficiency bonus, but a use is expended only if the attack hits. You regain all expended uses when you finish a long rest.
section | Membership in Knightly Orders

text | Knights of Solamnia are only ever members of one of their organization's orders. In the course of their training, most knights begin as members of the Knights of the Crown and then move on to join other orders. Whether a character follows this path or another, they retain what they learned as a member of an order even if they join another. Characters can change what knightly order they're a part of, but they always have access to any Knight of Solamnia feats they've acquired.

title: Stone-Hard
contents: property | Type/Prerequisites | Virtues of the Dwarves Feat (Dwarven)
rule

text | The Dwarves were made to be strong to endure, for they came unto the world when a great evil ruled Middle-earth. As long as your spirit supports your strength you can resist any hurt of body more hardily than most.
bullet | Increase your Constitution score by 1, to a maximum of 20.
bullet | When you are reduced to 0 hit points but not killed outright, you can drop to 1 hit point instead. You cannot use this feature again until you finish a short or long rest.

title: Stout-Hearted
contents: property | Type/Prerequisites | Virtues of the Bardings Feat (Barding)
rule
text | Living for many a year under the ever-present menace of Smaug the Dreadful has made the Bardings a courageous race. After all, there are not many creatures more fearsome than a great Dragon...
bullet | Increase your Wisdom or Charisma by 1, to a maximum of 20.
bullet | You have advantage on Charisma saving throws, and you cannot be frightened by non-undead creatures.

title: Strange as News from Bree
contents: property | Type/Prerequisites | Virtues of the Men of Bree Feat (Men of Bree/Bree-Hobbit)
rule

text | It is indeed true that the affairs of the four villages are the chief concern of the Bree-folk, but the most inquisitive among the inhabitants are always ready to gather what tidings come from distant lands. You have learnt that listening to the strange tales that travellers often talk about can prove to be very precious.
bullet | Increase your Intelligence or Wisdom by 1, to a maximum of 20.
bullet | You gain proficiency in the Investigation skill. If you are already proficient, you add double your proficiency bonus to checks you make with it.
bullet | During each Fellowship Phase, you can receive a rumour from the Loremaster.

title: Street Justice
contents: property | Type/Prerequisites | General Feat (Lvl 4)
rule
text | You gain the following benefits.
description | Ability Score Increase | Increase your Strength or Dexterity by 1, to a maximum of 20.
description | Headlock | Your allies have Advantage on attack rolls against a creature Grappled by you.
description | Sturdy Knot | When you use Chain, Manacles, or Rope to bind a creature, add your Proficiency Bonus to the DC to escape or burst the Chain, Manacles, or Rope.
description | Tough Talk | A creature's Hostile attitude doesn't impose Disadvantage on your Charisma (Intimidation) checks to influence that creature.

title: Strength of Will
contents: property | Type/Prerequisites | Virtues of the Rangers of the North Feat (Ranger of the North)
rule

text | You have been raised in a land dotted with desolate ruins and burial mounds. You have heard the dead whisper with cold voices out of lonely barrows, while ghostly lights dance on haunted hills. When your will strengthens your heart, there is hardly anything in this world that can dismay you.
bullet | Increase your Wisdom or Charisma by 1, to a maximum of 20.
bullet | You have advantage on Charisma saving throws.
bullet | Whenever you must make a Charisma saving throw, you gain a bonus to the saving throw equal to your Wisdom modifier (minimum +1).

title: Strike of the Giants
contents: property | Type/Prerequisites | Prof m. weapons/Giant Foundling
rule

text | You have absorbed primeval magic that gives you an echo of the might of giants. When you take this feat, choose one of the benefits listed below. Once per turn, when you hit a target with a melee weapon attack or a ranged weapon attack using a thrown weapon, you can imbue the attack with an additional effect depending on the benefit you chose:

description | Cloud Strike | The target takes an extra 1d4 thunder damage. If the target is a creature, it must succeed on a Wisdom saving throw, or you become invisible to it until the start of your next turn or until immediately after you make an attack roll or cast a spell.
description | Fire Strike | The target takes an extra 1d10 fire damage.

description | Frost Strike | The target takes an extra 1d6 cold damage. If the target is a creature, it must succeed on a Constitution saving throw, or its speed is reduced to 0 until the start of your next turn.
description | Hill Strike | The target takes an extra 1d6 damage of the weapon's type. If the target is a creature, it must succeed on a Strength saving throw or have the prone condition.
description | Stone Strike | The target takes an extra 1d6 force damage. If the target is a creature, it must succeed on a Strength saving throw or be pushed 10 feet from you in a straight line.

description | Storm Strike | The target takes an extra 1d6 lightning damage. If the target is a creature, it must succeed on a Constitution saving throw, or it has disadvantage on attack rolls until the start of your next turn.
text | The saving throw DC for these effects equals 8 + your proficiency bonus + your Strength or Constitution modifier.
text | You can use this feat a number of times equal to your proficiency bonus, and you regain all expended uses when you finish a long rest.

title: Strike of the Giants; Cloud
contents: property | Type/Prerequisites | Prof m. weapons/Giant Foundling
rule

text | You have absorbed primeval magic that gives you an echo of the might of giants. Once per turn, when you hit a target with a melee weapon attack or a ranged weapon attack using a thrown weapon, you can imbue the attack with an additional effect:

description | Cloud Strike | The target takes an extra 1d4 thunder damage. If the target is a creature, it must succeed on a Wisdom saving throw, or you become invisible to it until the start of your next turn or until immediately after you make an attack roll or cast a spell.

title: Strike of the Giants; Fire
contents: property | Type/Prerequisites | Prof m. weapons/Giant Foundling
rule

text | You have absorbed primeval magic that gives you an echo of the might of giants. Once per turn, when you hit a target with a melee weapon attack or a ranged weapon attack using a thrown weapon, you can imbue the attack with an additional effect:
description | Fire Strike | The target takes an extra 1d10 fire damage.

title: Strike of the Giants; Frost
contents: property | Type/Prerequisites | Prof m. weapons/Giant Foundling
rule

text | You have absorbed primeval magic that gives you an echo of the might of giants. Once per turn, when you hit a target with a melee weapon attack or a ranged weapon attack using a thrown weapon, you can imbue the attack with an additional effect:

description | Frost Strike | The target takes an extra 1d6 cold damage. If the target is a creature, it must succeed on a Constitution saving throw, or its speed is reduced to 0 until the start of your next turn.

title: Strike of the Giants; Hill
contents: property | Type/Prerequisites | Prof m. weapons/Giant Foundling
rule

text | You have absorbed primeval magic that gives you an echo of the might of giants. Once per turn, when you hit a target with a melee weapon attack or a ranged weapon attack using a thrown weapon, you can imbue the attack with an additional effect:
description | Hill Strike | The target takes an extra 1d6 damage of the weapon's type. If the target is a creature, it must succeed on a Strength saving throw or have the prone condition.

title: Strike of the Giants; Stone
contents: property | Type/Prerequisites | Prof m. weapons/Giant Foundling
rule

text | You have absorbed primeval magic that gives you an echo of the might of giants. Once per turn, when you hit a target with a melee weapon attack or a ranged weapon attack using a thrown weapon, you can imbue the attack with an additional effect:
description | Stone Strike | The target takes an extra 1d6 force damage. If the target is a creature, it must succeed on a Strength saving throw or be pushed 10 feet from you in a straight line.

title: Strike of the Giants; Storm
contents: property | Type/Prerequisites | Prof m. weapons/Giant Foundling
rule

text | You have absorbed primeval magic that gives you an echo of the might of giants. Once per turn, when you hit a target with a melee weapon attack or a ranged weapon attack using a thrown weapon, you can imbue the attack with an additional effect:

description | Storm Strike | The target takes an extra 1d6 lightning damage. If the target is a creature, it must succeed on a Constitution saving throw, or it has disadvantage on attack rolls until the start of your next turn.
text | The saving throw DC for these effects equals 8 + your proficiency bonus + your Strength or Constitution modifier.
text | You can use this feat a number of times equal to your proficiency bonus, and you regain all expended uses when you finish a long rest.

title: Strixhaven Initiate
contents: rule
text | You have studied some magical theory and have learned a few spells associated with Strixhaven University.

text | Choose one of Strixhaven's colleges: Lorehold, Prismari, Quandrix, Silverquill, or Witherbloom. You learn two cantrips and one 1st-level spell based on the college you choose, as specified in the Strixhaven Spells table.

text | You can cast the chosen 1st-level spell without a spell slot, and you must finish a long rest before you can cast it in this way again. You can also cast the spell using any spell slots you have.
text | Your spellcasting ability for this feat's spells is Intelligence, Wisdom, or Charisma (choose when you select this feat).
text | Strixhaven Spells
text | College｜Cantrips｜1st-Level Spell
text | Lorehold｜Choose two from light, sacred flame, and thaumaturgy.｜Choose one 1st-level cleric or wizard spell.
text | Prismari｜Choose two from fire bolt, prestidigitation, and ray of frost.｜Choose one 1st-level bard or sorcerer spell.
text | Quandrix｜Choose two from druidcraft, guidance, and mage hand.｜Choose one 1st-level druid or wizard spell.
text | Silverquill｜Choose two from sacred flame, thaumaturgy, and vicious mockery.｜Choose one 1st-level bard or cleric spell.
text | Witherbloom｜Choose two from chill touch, druidcraft, and spare the dying.｜Choose one 1st-level druid or wizard spell.

title: Strixhaven Mascot
contents: property | Type/Prerequisites | Lvl 4, Strixhaven Initiate
rule
text | You have learned how to summon a Strixhaven mascot to assist you, granting you these benefits:

bullet | You can cast the find familiar spell as a ritual. Your familiar can take the form of the mascot associated with the college you chose for the Strixhaven Initiate feat: a spirit statue mascot (Lorehold), an art elemental mascot (Prismari), a fractal mascot (Quandrix), an inkling mascot (Silverquill), or a pest mascot (Witherbloom).
bullet | When you take the Attack action on your turn, you can forgo one attack to allow your mascot familiar to make one attack of its own with its reaction.

bullet | If your mascot familiar is within 60 feet of you, you can teleport as an action, swapping places with the familiar. If your destination space is too small for you to occupy, the teleportation fails and is wasted. Once you teleport in this way, you can't do so again until you finish a long rest, unless you expend a spell slot of 2nd level or higher to do it again.

title: Sure at the Mark
contents: property | Type/Prerequisites | Virtues of the Hobbits Feat (Hobbit)
rule

text | Hobbits are nimble and keen-eyed, qualities that make them good archers. You have honed this natural talent to perfection, so much so that you just need to stoop for a stone to find yourself armed with a deadly weapon.
bullet | Increase your Dexterity score by 1, to a maximum of 20.
bullet | You are proficient with improvised weapons.
bullet | When making a ranged attack with an improvised weapon, you can use your choice of your Strength or your Dexterity modifier for the attack and damage rolls.

bullet | As a bonus action, you can give yourself advantage on your next ranged attack roll on the current turn. You can use this bonus action only if you haven't taken reactions during this turn, and after you use the bonus action, you cannot take reactions until the start of your next turn.

title: Survivor
contents: property | Type/Prerequisites | Origin Feat (—)
rule
text | Your ability to make the best out of a bad situation has served you well. You gain the following benefits.
description | Hardy | You need half the amount of food per day based on your size.
description | Intuitive | When you take the Study action, you have Advantage on Intelligence checks made when taking the Study action.
description | Shake It Off | Whenever you finish a Short Rest, your Exhaustion level, if any, decreases by 1. Additionally, when you finish a Long Rest, your Exhaustion level decreases by 2.

title: Svirfneblin Magic
contents: property | Type/Prerequisites | Gnome (deep)
rule

text | You have inherited the innate spellcasting ability of your ancestors. This ability allows you to cast nondetection on yourself at will, without needing a material component. You can also cast each of the following spells once with this ability: blindness/deafness, blur, and disguise self. You regain the ability to cast these spells when you finish a long rest.
text | Intelligence is your spellcasting ability for these spells, and you cast them at their lowest possible levels.

title: Swift Witchcraft
contents: property | Type/Prerequisites | General Feat (Lvl 4, Spellcasting)
rule
text | You have honed your skills in the rituals of witchcraft and can quickly weave your magic in times of need. You gain the following benefits.
description | Ability Score Increase | Increase your Intelligence, Wisdom, or Charisma by 1, to a maximum of 20.
description | Swift Invocation | You can use an action to cast a spell that has a casting time of 1 minute. Once you use this benefit, you can't do so again until you finish a Long Rest.

title: Syndicate Spy
contents: property | Type/Prerequisites | General Feat (Lvl 4, Syndicate Smuggler)
rule
text | Rising in the ranks of the Ebon Syndicate has taught you the clandestine skills of spycraft, valuable in your trade as a racketeer. You gain the following benefits.
description | Locksmith | You have proficiency with Thieves' Tools. While holding Thieves' Tools, you can craft a key to a lock by spending 10 minutes with that lock.

description | Master of Disguise | You have proficiency with a Disguise Kit. If you spend 10 minutes donning a Costume,  you decide what you look like, including your height, weight, facial features, sound of your voice, hair length, coloration, and other distinguishing characteristics. You can make yourself appear as a member of another species, though none of your statistics change. You can't appear as a creature of a different size, and your basic shape stays the same; if you're bipedal, you can't use this benefit to become quadrupedal, for instance.
description | Master Calligrapher | You have proficiency with a Forgery Kit. You aren't limited to 10 or fewer words when mimicking someone else's handwriting.

description | Blending In | When using the Hide action to conceal yourself from creatures that aren't Hostile to you, you need only be Lightly Obscured and you may use Charisma instead of Dexterity for the check.

title: Tavern Brawler
contents: property | Type/Prerequisites | Origin Feat (—)
rule
text | You gain the following benefits.

description | Enhanced Unarmed Strike | When you hit with your Unarmed Strike and deal damage, you can deal Bludgeoning damage equal to 1d4 plus your Strength modifier instead of the normal damage of an Unarmed Strike.
description | Damage Rerolls | Whenever you roll a damage die for your Unarmed Strike, you can reroll the die if it rolls a 1, and you must use the new roll.
description | Improvised Weaponry | You have proficiency with improvised weapons.

description | Push | When you hit a creature with an Unarmed Strike as part of the Attack action on your turn, you can deal damage to the target and also push it 5 feet away from you. You can use this benefit only once per turn.

title: Telekinetic
contents: property | Type/Prerequisites | General Feat (Lvl 4)
rule
text | You gain the following benefits.
description | Ability Score Increase | Increase your Intelligence, Wisdom, or Charisma by 1, to a maximum of 20.

description | Minor Telekinesis | You learn the Mage Hand spell. You can cast it without Verbal or Somatic components, you can make the spectral hand Invisible, and its range and the distance it can be away from you both increase by 30 feet when you cast it. The spell's spellcasting ability is the ability increased by this feat.

description | Telekinetic Shove | As a Bonus Action, you can telekinetically shove one creature you can see within 30 feet of yourself. When you do so, the target must succeed on a Strength saving throw (DC 8 plus the ability modifier of the score increased by this feat and your Proficiency Bonus) or be moved 5 feet toward or away from you.

title: Telepathic
contents: property | Type/Prerequisites | General Feat (Lvl 4)
rule
text | You gain the following benefits.
description | Ability Score Increase | Increase your Intelligence, Wisdom, or Charisma by 1, to a maximum of 20.

description | Telepathic Utterance | You can speak telepathically to any creature you can see within 60 feet of yourself. Your telepathic utterances are in a language you know, and the creature understands you only if it knows that language. Your communication doesn't give the creature the ability to respond to you telepathically.

description | Detect Thoughts | You always have the Detect Thoughts spell prepared. You can cast it without a spell slot or spell components, and you must finish a Long Rest before you can cast it in this way again. You can also cast it using spell slots you have of the appropriate level. Your spellcasting ability for the spell is the ability increased by this feat.

title: The Art of Smoking
contents: property | Type/Prerequisites | Virtues of the Men of Bree Feat (Men of Bree/Bree-Hobbit)
rule
text | You have mastered the art of smoking pipe-weed, and you always carry your pipe with you, as well as a bag of tobacco, for using it grants you patience and clarity of mind.
bullet | Increase your Intelligence, Wisdom, or Charisma by 1, to a maximum of 20.
bullet | Your proficiency bonus is doubled for any ability check you make with pipes.
bullet | You can use your pipe to gain inspiration a number of times equal to your proficiency bonus, instead of once, before you need to finish a long rest.

title: The Language of Birds
contents: property | Type/Prerequisites | Virtues of the Bardings Feat (Barding)
rule

text | Stories told in the North say how Dalemen can become so wise as to comprehend the speech of birds, and so by understanding their song be forewarned of impending threats, or be able to spot a weakness in their enemies' defences.
bullet | Increase your Intelligence or Wisdom by 1, to a maximum of 20.
bullet | You can comprehend and verbally communicate with birds, and you can obtain a magical success on any Intelligence (Nature) and Wisdom (Animal Handling) check related to birds.

bullet | When you are outside and you make an ability check, attack roll, or saving throw, you can do so with advantage. You can use this virtue a number of times equal to your proficiency bonus, and you regain all expended uses when you finish a long rest.

title: Three is Company
contents: property | Type/Prerequisites | Virtues of the Hobbits Feat (Hobbit)
rule

text | The Wise say that it is sometimes best to trust to friendship than to great wisdom. This is particularly true for Hobbits, as everyone knows that it is hardly possible to separate them from their friends. You have given your trust to your fellow Company members, and a true friendship has grown from the bond unifying you.
bullet | Increase your Charisma score by 1, to a maximum of 20.
bullet | Increase the Fellowship rating of your Company by 1.

bullet | Whenever you or a companion you can see spend a Fellowship point, choose a number of companions (which can include yourself) who can see or hear you, up to your proficiency bonus. Each of those companions gains inspiration.

title: Thrown Arms Master
contents: rule
text | You've honed your ability to lob weaponry into the fray, including weapons not meant for ranged combat. You gain the following benefits:
bullet | Increase your Strength or Dexterity by 1, to a maximum of 20.

bullet | Simple and martial melee weapons without the thrown property have the thrown property for you. One-handed weapons have a normal range of 20 feet and a long range of 60 feet, while two-handed weapons have a normal range of 15 feet and a long range of 30 feet.
bullet | Weapons that already have the thrown property increase their short range by 20 feet and their long range by 40 feet for you.

bullet | When you miss with a thrown weapon attack using a light weapon, the weapon returns to your grasp like a boomerang at the end of your turn, unless something prevents it from returning. You can catch and stow as many weapons as you threw in this way.

title: Thrown Weapon Fighting
contents: property | Type/Prerequisites | Fighting Style Feat (Fighting Style)
rule
text | When you hit with a ranged attack roll using a weapon that has the Thrown property, you gain a +2 bonus to the damage roll.

title: Thrown Weapon Master
contents: property | Type/Prerequisites | General Feat (Lvl 4 + Str 13+/Dex 13+)
rule
text | You excel at thrown weapons. You gain the following benefits.
description | Ability Score Increase | Increase your Strength or Dexterity by 1, to a maximum of 20.

description | Multithrow | After you take the Attack action to make a ranged attack roll using a Simple weapon that has Thrown, you can make two additional ranged attacks using Simple weapons that have Thrown as a Bonus Action.
description | Quick Hands | As a Bonus Action, you can retrieve and stow or wield all Simple weapons with Thrown that are within 5 feet of you. You need a free hand to perform this maneuver.
description | Returning | Simple weapons with Thrown that you are proficient with also have the Returning property (see Chapter 8: Advanced Weapons & Equipment).

title: Tireless Reveler
contents: property | Type/Prerequisites | Origin Feat (—)
rule

text | When an ally you can see within 60 feet of yourself expends Heroic Inspiration, you can gain Heroic Inspiration if you lack it. You can use this benefit a number of times equal to your Proficiency Bonus, and you regain all expended uses when you finish a Short or Long Rest.

title: Tough
contents: property | Type/Prerequisites | Origin Feat (—)
rule

text | Your Hit Point maximum increases by an amount equal to twice your character level when you gain this feat. Whenever you gain a character level thereafter, your Hit Point maximum increases by an additional 2 Hit Points.

title: Tough as Old Tree-Roots
contents: property | Type/Prerequisites | Virtues of the Hobbits Feat (Hobbit/Bree-Hobbit)
rule

text | They say that Hobbits are difficult to dismay or kill, and that they recover their health at a prodigious pace if allowed to enjoy some peace and quiet. You have discovered that you recover wonderfully from falls and bruises (at the cost of some considerable pain).
bullet | Increase your Constitution score by 1, to a maximum of 20.
bullet | When you would roll one or more Hit Dice to regain hit points, you instead use the highest number possible for each die.

title: Treacherous Allure
contents: property | Type/Prerequisites | General Feat (Lvl 4)
rule
text | You gain the following benefits.
description | Ability Score Increase | Increase your Intelligence, Wisdom, or Charisma by 1, to a maximum of 20.

description | Enchanting Presence | You always have the Charm Person spell prepared. You can cast it without a spell slot, and you must finish a Long Rest before you can cast it in this way again. You can also cast it using spell slots you have of the appropriate level. Your spellcasting ability for the spell is the ability increased by this feat.
description | Inevitable Betrayal | You have Advantage on attack rolls against creatures with the Charmed condition.

title: Triage Expert
contents: property | Type/Prerequisites | Origin Feat (—)
rule
text | You are trained in dealing with injuries on the battlefield, and with the saving of lives afterward. You gain the following benefits.
description | Bedside Manner | Whenever you cause a creature to regain Hit Points or the Blood and Bone benefit, you can roll one extra die and discard the lowest die.

description | Blood and Bone | Taking the Utilize action and expending the use of a Healer's Kit lets you heal a creature within 5 feet of you. The creature can expend and roll a Hit Point Die and regain that number of Hit Points.

title: Two-Weapon Fighting
contents: property | Type/Prerequisites | Fighting Style Feat (Fighting Style)
rule

text | When you make an extra attack as a result of using a weapon that has the Light property, you can add your ability modifier to the damage of that attack if you aren't already adding it to the damage.

title: Tyro of the Gauntlet
contents: property | Type/Prerequisites | Origin Feat (—)
rule
text | You gain the following benefits.

description | Stand as One | When an ally within 5 feet of you is subjected to an effect that would push or pull it, you can take a Reaction to prevent that ally from being pushed or pulled. To receive this benefit, the ally can't have the Incapacitated condition.
description | Vigilant | When you take the Ready action, the next attack roll made against you has Disadvantage before the start of your next turn.

title: Unarmed Fighting
contents: property | Type/Prerequisites | Fighting Style Feat (Fighting Style)
rule

text | When you hit with your Unarmed Strike and deal damage, you can deal Bludgeoning damage equal to 1d6 plus your Strength modifier instead of the normal damage of an Unarmed Strike. If you aren't holding any weapons or a Shield when you make the attack roll, the d6 becomes a d8.
text | At the start of each of your turns, you can deal 1d4 Bludgeoning damage to one creature Grappled by you.

title: Unreflected
contents: property | Type/Prerequisites | Origin Feat (—)
rule
text | Your image is separated from you, making you unable to cast a reflection or a shadow, but you're able to easily slip notice. You gain the following benefits.
description | Inscrutable | Creatures have Disadvantage on Wisdom (Insight) checks made against you.

description | Unremarkable | The loss of some part of yourself leaves you able to fade into the background. While you are within a group of at least two other creatures of your type and that there are no more than one size larger or smaller than you, you can take the Hide action from creatures outside that group by making a Charisma (Deception) check instead of Dexterity (Stealth), with no need for being behind cover or Heavily Obscured. If you are ever more than 5 feet from at least two other such creatures while hiding this way, you are revealed.

title: Untameable Spirit
contents: property | Type/Prerequisites | Virtues of the Dwarves Feat (Dwarven)
rule
text | Dwarves were made from their beginning to resist any form of domination. You have strengthened your resolve against all but the most subtle of the weapons of the Enemy.
bullet | Increase your Intelligence or Wisdom by 1, to a maximum of 20.
bullet | You are immune to being charmed.
bullet | You have advantage on Intelligence saving throws.

title: Vampire Hunter
contents: property | Type/Prerequisites | Origin Feat (—)
rule
text | You gain the following benefits.
description | Adroit Escape | You have Advantage on checks to escape from nonmagical restraints or the Grappled condition.

description | Vitality Ward | When you take Necrotic damage, you can take a Reaction to mitigate the damage. Roll a number of d6s equal to your Proficiency Bonus, and add them together. Reduce the Necrotic damage you take by this total. Once you use this benefit, you can't use it again until you finish a Short or Long Rest.

title: Vampire Touched
contents: property | Type/Prerequisites | General Feat (Lvl 4)
rule
text | You gain the following benefits.
description | Ability Score Increase | Increase your Intelligence, Wisdom, or Charisma by 1, to a maximum of 20.
