description | Vampire Magic | Choose one level 1 spell from the Enchantment or Illusion school of magic. You always have that spell and the Spider Climb spell prepared. You can cast each of these spells without expending a spell slot, but when you cast Spider Climb this way, you must target yourself, and you must finish a Long Rest before you can cast each spell in this way again. You can also cast either spell using spell slots you have of the appropriate level. Your spellcasting ability for the spells is the ability increased by this feat.

title: Vampire's Plaything
contents: property | Type/Prerequisites | Origin Feat (—)
rule
text | You gain the following benefits.

description | Decanting | When you finish a Long Rest, you can create one Potion of Healing or an Antitoxin, as long as you have an empty vial or flask. These liquids evaporate when you finish another Long Rest.

description | Timely Retreat | You can take a Bonus Action to take the Dash action or the Disengage action. You can use this benefit a number of times equal to your Proficiency Bonus, and you recover all expended uses when you finish a Long Rest.

description | Vampiric Connection | The DM determines the fate of your former vampire master. While you and your former vampire master are on the same plane of existence, the vampire can communicate with you telepathically, and you can choose to allow the vampire to perceive through your senses.

title: Vigor of the Hill Giant
contents: property | Type/Prerequisites | Lvl 4, Strike of the Giants
rule
text | You've manifested the resilience emblematic of hill giants, granting you the following benefits:
bullet | Ability Score Increase. Increase your Strength, Constitution, or Wisdom by 1, to a maximum of 20.

bullet | Bulwark. When you are subjected to an effect that would move you at least 5 feet or give you the prone condition, you can use your reaction to steady yourself. You aren't moved and don't have the prone condition.

bullet | Iron Stomach. Whenever you eat food as part of a short rest and spend one or more Hit Dice to regain hit points, you regain additional hit points equal to your Constitution modifier + your proficiency bonus.

title: Virtue
contents: property | Type/Prerequisites | Beyond 10th Level Feat (—)
rule
text | You gain a common or cultural virtue of your choice (see Chapter 4).

title: Vital Sacrifice
contents: rule

text | You've learned secrets of hemocraft that grant you esoteric power at the price of your own life force. As a bonus action, you can choose to take 1d6 necrotic damage to gain a blood boon. Your blood boon lasts for 1 hour or until expended.
text | You can expend this blood boon to gain one of the following benefits:
bullet | When you make an attack roll, you roll 1d6 and add it to the total.
bullet | When you hit with an attack or spell, you deal an additional 2d6 necrotic damage.
bullet | When you cause a creature to make a Strength, Dexterity, or Constitution saving throw, roll a d4 and reduce their save by the amount rolled.
text | The damage you take to gain a blood boon can't be reduced in any way.

title: War Caster
contents: property | Type/Prerequisites | General Feat (Lvl 4, Spellcasting)
rule
text | You gain the following benefits.
description | Ability Score Increase | Increase your Intelligence, Wisdom, or Charisma by 1, to a maximum of 20.
description | Concentration | You have Advantage on Constitution saving throws that you make to maintain Concentration.

description | Reactive Spell | When a creature provokes an Opportunity Attack from you by leaving your reach, you can take a Reaction to cast a spell at the creature rather than making an Opportunity Attack. The spell must have a casting time of one action and must target only that creature.
description | Somatic Components | You can perform the Somatic components of spells even when you have weapons or a Shield in one or both hands.

title: Ways of the Wild
contents: property | Type/Prerequisites | Virtues of the Rangers of the North Feat (Ranger of the North)
rule

text | The Rangers of the North roam ceaselessly, hunting for wicked things and guarding all frontiers. For you the Wild has become as familiar as the road that leads home is familiar to Hobbits, and the land itself may reveal useful tidings.
bullet | Increase your Wisdom score by 1, to a maximum of 20.
bullet | During a journey, you can take on multiple roles without penalties.

bullet | You gain proficiency in one of the following skills of your choice: Explore, Hunting, or Travel. If you are already proficient in each of them, you add double your proficiency bonus to checks you make with one of them of your choice.

bullet | When you make a Wisdom check using a skill in which you are proficient, you can obtain a magical success. You can do so a number of times equal to your proficiency bonus, and you regain all expended uses when you finish a long rest.

title: Weapon Master
contents: property | Type/Prerequisites | General Feat (Lvl 4)
rule
text | You gain the following benefits.
description | Ability Score Increase | Increase your Strength or Dexterity by 1, to a maximum of 20.

description | Mastery Property | Your training with weapons allows you to use the mastery property of one kind of Simple or Martial weapon of your choice, provided you have proficiency with it. Whenever you finish a Long Rest, you can change the kind of weapon to another eligible kind.

title: Weapon-Craft
contents: property | Type/Prerequisites | Craft Feat (—)
rule

text | Choose a type of weapon. You gain proficiency with this weapon if you don't already have it. When you attack with that type of weapon and the weapon is finesse or ranged, you can use your choice of your Intelligence, Wisdom, or Charisma modifier instead of your Strength or Dexterity modifier for the attack and damage rolls. You must use the same modifier for both rolls.

text | In addition, when you hit a creature with the chosen weapon, you can spend a craft slot to roll a number of additional weapon damage dice on the damage roll. The number of additional weapon damage dice is equal to your proficiency bonus.

title: Witch Hunter
contents: property | Type/Prerequisites | General Feat (Lvl 4)
rule
text | You have honed and perfected your skills fighting against spellcasters, possibly from your background hunting mages as part of the Arcanist Inquisition. You gain the following benefits.
description | Ability Score Increase | Increase your Strength, Constitution, or Wisdom by 1, to a maximum of 20.

description | Dodge Spells | You can take a Reaction to avoid a spell that is targeting only you and doesn't create an area of effect. Make a Wisdom saving throw against the spellcaster's spell save DC. On a successful save, the creature must choose a new target or the spell is canceled. A canceled spell dissipates with no effect, and any resources used to cast it are wasted.

description | Keep Your Enemies Close | When you hit a target that can cast spells with an attack roll using a Melee weapon or an Unarmed Strike, you can reduce its Speed by 15 feet until the start of your next turn.
description | Resist Curses | You have Advantage on saving throws against all Shadowsteel curses, and also against spells with a duration of more than 10 minutes.

title: Wood Elf Magic
contents: property | Type/Prerequisites | Elf (wood)
rule

text | You learn the magic of the primeval woods, which are revered and protected by your people. You learn one druid cantrip of your choice. You also learn the longstrider and pass without trace spells, each of which you can cast once without expending a spell slot. You regain the ability to cast these two spells in this way when you finish a long rest. Wisdom is your spellcasting ability for all three spells.

title: Wood-Craft
contents: property | Type/Prerequisites | Craft Feat (—)
rule

text | You are familiar with the difficulties encountered when crossing wild lands, and with the ways of overcoming them. You can make an Intelligence (Nature) check in place of any Wisdom (Explore) or Wisdom (Hunting) check.

text | In addition, you can spend 1 hour and a craft slot to make an Intelligence (Nature), Wisdom (Explore), or Wisdom (Hunting) check (your choice). The DC is determined by the terrain type the Company is currently traversing (as if a journey event was taking place there; see Chapter 5). The check has disadvantage if it is autumn or winter. If you succeed, choose one of the following options:

bullet | Choose a number of creatures equal to your check result minus the terrain DC (minimum 1), which can include yourself. For 1 hour, each of those creatures has a +5 bonus to its Dexterity (Stealth) checks. When you reach 5th level, the bonus increases to +10. If you obtain a magical success, the creatures also leave behind no tracks or other traces of their passage for the duration.

bullet | Choose a number of creatures equal to your check result minus the terrain DC (minimum 1), which can include yourself. For 8 hours, each of those creatures has a +5 bonus to its passive Wisdom (Perception) score. When you reach 5th level, the bonus increases to +10. If you obtain a magical success, the creatures also gain tremorsense out to a range of 60 feet for the duration.

bullet | You forage a number of pounds of food and gallons of water equal to your check result minus the terrain DC (minimum 1). The food lasts for 24 hours, or 48 hours when you reach 5th level. If you obtain a magical success, each pound of food foraged in this way also restores 1 hit point to the creature eating it during a short or long rest.

title: Woodwise
contents: rule
text | You have lived your entire life in the gnarled, wooded areas of the world. You are adept at finding your way through even the most treacherous terrain. You gain the following benefits:
bullet | You gain proficiency in either the Survival or Nature skill.
bullet | You ignore difficult terrain.
bullet | You cannot become lost in natural surroundings except by magical means.

title: Zhentarim Ruffian
contents: property | Type/Prerequisites | Origin Feat (—)
rule
text | You gain the following benefits.
description | Exploit Opening | When you roll damage for an Opportunity Attack, you can roll the damage dice twice and use either roll against the target.
description | Family First | If you have Heroic Inspiration when you roll Initiative, you can expend it to give yourself and your allies Advantage on that Initiative roll.

title: Zhentarim Tactics
contents: property | Type/Prerequisites | General Feat (Lvl 4, Zhentarim Ruffian)
rule
text | You gain the following benefits.
description | Ability Score Increase | Increase your Dexterity or Charisma by 1, to a maximum of 20.
description | Retaliate | Immediately after a creature within 5 feet of you hits you with a melee attack, you can make an Opportunity Attack against that creature.
description | Versatile Merc | When you finish a Long Rest, choose a skill in which you have proficiency. You have Expertise in that skill until you finish your next Long Rest.
